Это is the neuter demonstrative subject 'this/that', and подозлило is a neuter singular past tense verb agreeing with это. Тотчас же is an adverbial phrase meaning 'immediately' with же adding emphasis; the whole order (object + subject + adverb + verb) stresses the speaker's feeling that 'this' instantly made him angry. Note: подозлило here is an older sense meaning 'to anger/vex' rather than 'to suspect'.
segments.p2-6-g336: Как as a one-word exclamation expresses surprise or indignation ('What!' / 'How!'). It is elliptical here (no verb) and functions as an interjection rather than a relative/comparative word.
segments.p2-6-g337: я is the subject 'I'. так 'so' modifies the manner (so/generally 'that much'). Было is the past neuter particle often used parenthetically to mark a fleeting or tentative action ('had been' / 'for a moment was'), so так было кротко conveys 'I was (for a moment) so gentle'. Кротко is an adverb ('gently' / 'meekly'), and с ней is the prepositional phrase 'with her' giving the object/partner of the behavior.
segments.p2-6-g338: А is a contrastive particle here ('and/but'), introducing the contrastive clause that follows; она 'she' is left elliptical (the verb is omitted) to trail off, implying 'and she [did something different]' or 'and she (did not reciprocate)'.
segments.p2-6-g341: Да here is a modal/exclamatory particle ('oh/come on' tone) rather than a simple 'yes'. Ты is the 2nd-person singular subject 'you'. Что is an exclamatory particle ('what' — used colloquially to express incredulity). Думаешь is present-tense, 2nd-person singular of 'думать' ('do you think/are you thinking') and forms the question; intonation makes it rhetorical/expressive.
segments.p2-6-g242: "В чахотке" uses the preposition в + prepositional case to indicate the condition/cause ('in/with consumption') rather than a literal location; this is an idiomatic way to say 'of consumption'. "померла" is the past tense, feminine singular form of помереть (perfective), colloquial for 'died' — a simple past statement about a female subject.
segments.p2-6-g243: Девка is a colloquial/rough word for 'girl' used as the subject. "в больнице" is prepositional case with в indicating location ('in the hospital'). "бы померла" combines the particle бы (conditional marker) with the past form померла to form a counterfactual/hypothetical past meaning 'would have died'.
segments.p2-6-g246: Она (she) is the subject; уж is an adverbial particle meaning 'already' (or emphatic 'indeed') that precedes the object phrase; об этом is the preposition об + prepositional pronoun 'this' and functions as the thing known; знает is 3rd person singular present indicative 'knows' or 'is aware'. The clause is a present-tense remark about her current knowledge.
segments.p2-6-g247: подумал is the past tense, masculine singular of подумать ('I thought'); it functions here as a parenthetical narration of the speaker's internal comment. The subject pronoun я follows the verb (inversion typical in Russian narrative) but still refers to the narrator.
segments.p2-6-g248: и is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this remark to what follows; сказала is past feminine singular of сказать ('said') and here introduces direct reported speech (what was actually spoken). The feminine form refers back to the previously mentioned female subject.
segments.p2-6-g249: девка is the quoted word; it is colloquial and somewhat coarse for 'girl' or 'maid' and is presented as the exact phrasing she used. As quoted speech it signals register contrast with the more polite девушка.
segments.p2-6-g250: а не is a contrastive construction meaning 'and not' / 'rather than'; а here marks contrast ('but/rather'), не is simple negation, and девушка is the standard polite word 'young woman' or 'miss' being negated — so the phrase corrects the register: 'devka, not devushka.'
segments.p2-6-g251: The clause has an introductory dash. Она is the subject 'she'; хозяйке is dative singular of хозяйка ('to the mistress/landlady') indicating the party to whom she owed something or was obliged; должна была is a past verbal-adjectival construction (должна + past была) expressing past obligation/indebtedness — literally 'was obliged/owed' (feminine).
segments.p2-6-g252: возразил is the past tense, masculine singular of возразить ('objected, retorted'); it functions as the narrator's speech tag. The pronoun я follows the verb (inverted order) and identifies the speaker.
segments.p2-6-g253: все более и более is a fixed intensifying phrase meaning 'more and more' or 'increasingly' (все here intensifies 'more'); подзадориваясь is a present active gerund (деепричастие) from подзадориваться meaning 'becoming/go[ing]aded' or 'getting provoked/encouraged' and modifies the subject of the main clause (I), describing how the speaker spoke; спором is the instrumental singular 'by/with the argument', showing cause/means.
segments.p2-6-g254: и links this continuation; до самого почти конца is a temporal phrase: до ('until/up to') + самого ('the very' — intensifier in genitive agreeing with конца) + почти ('almost') + конца (genitive of конец 'end') giving 'almost until the very end'; ей служила is past feminine служила ('served') with the dative ей indicating the beneficiary ('she served her').
segments.p2-6-g255: хоть и is a concessive conjunction meaning 'although/even though' (хоть with the particle и adding emphasis); в чахотке is the same prepositional phrase as earlier indicating condition/cause ('with consumption/consumptive'), and была is past feminine of быть ('was') — together: 'though she was consumptive.'
segments.p2-6-g306: Привяжется is the future, 3rd-person singular reflexive form of привязаться (perfective), here meaning 'become attached' or 'cling' (not literal tying). It functions as the main verb of the first clause and implies a spontaneous/resultant attachment.
segments.p2-6-g307: Так is a short adverb/particle meaning 'then' or 'in that case' here; it links the first clause to the result clause that follows rather than describing manner.
segments.p2-6-g308: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'probably' that softens the assertion; it modifies the speaker's degree of certainty about the following statement.
segments.p2-6-g309: И is the coordinating particle 'and' used here in the fixed cluster (так и не) that gives a sense of final/resultant negation; не is the negation particle. Отвяжется is the future, 3rd-person singular reflexive of отвязаться (perfective) meaning 'be shaken off / free oneself' — together 'и не отвяжется' = 'and (so) will not get rid of it / will not let go.'
segments.p2-6-g310: Вот is a discourse particle here ('well/there') used to present the result or conclusion; и is the coordinating particle 'and' that often appears in colloquial resultatives (Вот и ... = 'and so/there you are'); помрешь is future, 2nd-person singular of помереть (perfective), meaning 'you will die.' The construction states the speaker's blunt conclusion about the listener.
segments.p2-6-g311: Ну is an interjection meaning 'well' that frames a resigned or irritated response; и here is an emphatic particle ('and/indeed') often used in colloquial phrases like 'ну и ...' to add force; помру is 1st-person singular future of помереть (perfective) — 'I will die.'
segments.p2-6-g312: Ответила is the feminine past tense of ответить ('replied'), agreeing with она. Совсем уж is a colloquial double intensifier ('completely/quite/utterly') modifying злобно ('spitefully, maliciously'). И coordinates the next verb; быстро modifies пошевельнулась, which is past reflexive (пошевельнуться) meaning 'moved/stirred slightly' or 'made a small motion.' The sequence gives manner and timing of her reaction.
segments.p2-6-g313: Да here functions as a particle equivalent to 'yes' or a mild contrastive 'well/yes'; ведь is a particle meaning 'after all' or 'you know,' adding justificatory emphasis; жалко is an impersonal predicative meaning 'it's a pity' or 'one feels sorry (for it).' Together they express sympathetic resignation.
segments.p2-6-g363: This is the idiomatic phrase 'не в пример' + comparative 'лучше'. 'Не' negates literally, but here with the prepositional phrase 'в пример' ('by example/comparison') it forms the frozen idiom 'не в пример' meaning 'beyond comparison' or 'by far'. 'Лучше' is the short-form comparative (adverbial/predicative) of 'хороший' meaning 'better'.
segments.p2-6-g364: 'А' is a contrastive/connecting particle ('and/but'). 'С любовью' is an instrumental phrase meaning 'with love' (the preposition 'с' + noun in instrumental). The conjunction/particle 'и' here has an emphatic sense ('even/also') linking to 'без счастья' ('without happiness'). 'Можно' is an impersonal modal ('it is possible' / 'one can'), and it governs the infinitive 'прожить' ('to live' / 'to get by'); together they create an impersonal construction meaning 'one can live'.
segments.p2-6-g365: 'И' simply links the thought ('and/even'). 'В горе' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in sorrow' (here 'горе' = sorrow, an abstract noun in the prepositional case). 'Жизнь' is the subject 'life', and 'хороша' is the short-form adjective meaning 'is good' (predicate adjective).
segments.p2-6-g366: 'Хорошо' is an adverb meaning 'well' / 'it is good'; here it introduces an evaluative clause. 'Жить' is the infinitive 'to live' (what is good). 'На свете' is a common locative expression meaning 'in the world' ('на' + prepositional 'свете' used idiomatically).
segments.p2-6-g367: 'Даже' is an intensifier 'even'. The sequence 'как бы ни' is a concessive particle combination ('however, no matter how') where 'как' = 'how', 'бы' is the modal/conditional particle, and 'ни' adds the concessive/negative force; together with the infinitive 'жить' it yields 'however one may live' or 'no matter how one lives'.
segments.p2-6-g368: 'А' again contrasts/continues ('and/but'). 'Здесь' means 'here'. 'Что' is an interrogative/exclamatory 'what' used rhetorically to ask 'what is there' or 'what (is to be said)', often leaving the thought unfinished.
segments.p2-6-g369: 'Кроме' is a preposition/particle meaning 'except' or 'besides'; here it stands alone at the end of the sentence, trailing to mean 'apart from that' or 'except ...' and signals that the speaker leaves the exception unspoken.
segments.p2-6-g372: A single noun used as an exclamatory utterance. Смрада is a colloquial/expressive form of смрад (stink) and here stands alone in the nominative (used like an interjection) to declare disgust.
segments.p2-6-g373: A blunt, vulgar interjection. Фуй is an expletive (archaic/vulgar in literary Russian) used to express contempt or disgust; grammatically it functions as an isolated interjection with no syntactic attachment.
segments.p2-6-g374: Повepнулся is a past-tense, perfective, reflexive verb meaning 'turned (around/away)' indicating a completed physical action. The phrase с омерзеньем uses the instrumental case (с + noun in instrumental) to indicate manner or accompaniment: 'with disgust.'
segments.p2-6-g375: Уже не forms a negation meaning 'no longer' here; холодно is an adverb of manner ('coldly' in an emotional sense) modifying the verb резонерствовал. Резонерствовал is past imperfective, derogatory in meaning ('to pontificate/moralize'), so the clause says he ceased to be detachedly or coldly pontificating.
segments.p2-6-g376: Начинал чувствовать is past progressive (imperfective) meaning 'began to feel.' Сам is an emphatic reflexive/pronominal adjective here meaning 'myself' (emphasizing the subject). Together this emphasizes that the speaker personally began to notice a change in his feelings.
segments.p2-6-g377: Что introduces a subordinate clause ('that') and is followed by говорю in the present tense. Russian often uses the present to report a current utterance or the content of speech; here it means 'that I am saying' or more naturally 'that I was saying' in context.
segments.p2-6-g378: И simply links the previous idea ('and'). Горячился is a past imperfective reflexive verb meaning 'became heated' or 'grew hot-tempered' (i.e., became emotionally agitated). It indicates an internal rise of passion accompanying the realization just described.
segments.p2-6-g342: На is the preposition 'on' governing the prepositional case (дороге). хорошей is the feminine singular prepositional form of хороший agreeing with дороге. The pronoun ты is unusually inserted between adjective and noun for colloquial emphasis (addressing 'you' inside the noun phrase). The whole phrase literally means 'on a good road' but with a pointed 'you' in the middle.
segments.p2-6-g343: А here is a short interrogative tag/particle (colloquial) asking for confirmation or expressing surprise — 'eh?/are you?'. It's not a logical 'but' here but a discourse particle.
segments.p2-6-g344: Ничего is fronted for emphasis and functions as the object meaning 'nothing/anything' in negative contexts (ничего ... не думаю = 'don't think anything'). The clause follows with the pronoun я and the negated present verb не думаю ('I do not think'). Word order stresses the negated object.
segments.p2-6-g345: То is the demonstrative neuter pronoun meaning 'that' (referring to the preceding idea). И here functions as an intensifying/colloquial particle ('indeed/precisely') rather than a plain coordinating 'and'. Худо is a predicative (adverbial/pseudo-adjective) meaning 'bad' or 'woeful' — the phrase means 'that is precisely what is bad.'
segments.p2-6-g346: Что introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that' (explaining what is bad). Не думаешь is the present-second-person singular negative ('you do not think'), giving the content of the subordinate clause.
segments.p2-6-g347: Очнись is the imperative singular of очнуться ('to come to one's senses' / 'wake up'), with the reflexive suffix -сь; it's a direct, colloquial command addressed to one person.
segments.p2-6-g348: Пока introduces a temporal clause meaning 'while' or 'as long as' (here 'while/while there is still time'). Время есть uses the present form есть (from быть) as an existential 'there is' — literally 'time exists' = 'there is time' (often understood as 'still time').
segments.p2-6-g349: А here is a contrastive particle meaning 'but' or 'yet' that links this remark to the previous sentence. время is the noun 'time' in the nominative and functions as the subject; -то is the emphatic particle attached to время to stress its existence ('indeed/after all'). есть is the present tense of быть used existentially ('there is/exists').
segments.p2-6-g350: Ты is the familiar singular 'you'. ещё is an adverb 'still/yet' modifying the predicate. молода is a short-form adjective (feminine) used predicatively: 'are young.' The short form places emphasis on state rather than attributive description.
segments.p2-6-g351: собой here is a reflexive pronoun in an oblique (instrumental-like) sense used idiomatically with хорoша to mean 'good-looking' or 'fine in yourself' (roughly 'in appearance'). хороша is the short-form feminine adjective serving as the predicate ('you are good/pretty').
segments.p2-6-g352: могла is the past tense, feminine form of мочь used here with бы to form a counterfactual/conditional 'could' (могла бы = 'you could'). полюбить is a perfective infinitive 'to fall in love' (or 'to come to love'). The construction expresses potential action she might take.
segments.p2-6-g353: замуж is an adverbial/particle used with verbs to indicate marrying (for a woman). пойти is an infinitive 'to go' used idiomatically with замуж (замуж пойти) meaning 'to get married' or 'to go off to a husband's home.'
segments.p2-6-g354: счастливой is the short-form/adjective in feminine that names the state 'happy' (here functioning as a predicative complement). быть is an infinitive 'to be' following the list of possibles: 'to be happy' — indicating a potential resulting state.
segments.p2-6-g357: Не все is the negation 'not all' ('not everyone'). замужем is a predicative short-form/adverb meaning 'being married' (used of a woman). The enclitic -то after замужем is emphatic, softening or stressing the word; счастливые is a nominative plural adjective 'happy' describing those married. The word order is colloquial and elliptical.
segments.p2-6-g358: отрезала is past tense, feminine of отрезать used figuratively 'cut off' or 'snapped (out)' — here meaning she interrupted or spoke sharply. она is the subject pronoun 'she.' прежней and грубой are adjectives in the instrumental that modify скороговоркой; скороговоркой (instrumental singular) literally 'by/with a tongue-twister' is used metaphorically to mean a quick, clipped manner of speaking.
segments.p2-6-g359: Не все is again the short negation 'not all'/'not everyone' given ellipitcally as an answer fragment. The phrase leaves the noun understood from context.
segments.p2-6-g360: конечно is an adverbial parenthetical 'of course' or 'naturally' qualifying her concession.
segments.p2-6-g361: а is a contrastive conjunction 'but.' все-таки is an idiomatic adverb formed from всё + таки meaning 'after all'/'all the same' or 'still' (всё and таки appear separately here); лучше is the comparative 'better,' and гораздо is an adverbial intensifier 'much' or 'far' modifying лучше.
segments.p2-6-g362: чем introduces the standard of comparison 'than.' здесь is the adverb 'here' (meaning 'in this place/situation' i.e., the present circumstances). Together they form the comparative clause 'than here.'
segments.p2-6-g379: Subject 'Я' begins the clause; 'уже' is an adverb ('already') that frames the timing of the speaker's state; 'свои' is the reflexive possessive agreeing with the following noun and means 'my own'; 'заветные' is an attributive adjective meaning 'cherished' or 'treasured'; 'идейки' is a diminutive plural of 'ideas' (colloquial, 'little ideas'). The whole fragment is the direct object phrase of the verb that follows.
segments.p2-6-g380: 'в углу' is a locative prepositional phrase ('in the corner') modifying the preceding noun phrase; 'выжитые' is a past participle/Adjective agreeing in number and gender with 'идейки' and here has a figurative sense — ideas that have been kept, hardened or 'made to survive' in a corner. The comma shows this is a parenthetic participial modifier.
segments.p2-6-g381: 'жаждал' is the past-tense form of the imperfective verb 'жаждать' meaning 'to long for' or 'to be eager for'; it governs the infinitive 'изложить' (perfective) which names what he longed to do — 'to set forth' or 'to present' the ideas. Together they form 'I longed to set forth...'.
segments.p2-6-g382: 'Что-то' is an indefinite pronoun ('something'); 'вдруг' is an adverb ('suddenly'); 'во мне' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'within me' (literally 'in me'); 'загорелось' is the past tense (perfective-like in use) of 'загореться' and is figurative here — 'caught fire', i.e., enthusiasm or emotion was kindled in me.
segments.p2-6-g383: 'какая-то' is an indefinite phrase 'some kind of'; 'цель' is the noun 'goal' or 'aim'; 'явилась' is past tense of the perfective 'явиться' meaning 'appeared' or 'presented itself' — the quotation marks around 'явилась' in the original mark the appearance as sudden or almost ironic.
segments.p2-6-g384: Imperative addressed to 'ты' (you singular): 'не смотри' is a negative imperative ('do not look/consider'); 'на меня' marks the object or target of that looking/considering ('at me' / 'to me'). The verb 'смотри' here carries the sense 'regard/consider' rather than merely 'look physically'.
segments.p2-6-g385: Conjunction 'что' here functions as 'that' or 'the fact that' (not an interrogative); 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'здесь' is an adverb of place 'here' — together this fragment gives the reason the addressee might be tempted to regard the speaker ('that I am here').
segments.p2-6-g386: Standard predicative construction 'я ... не пример': 'я' (I) + dative 'тебе' ('to you' / 'for you') + negation 'не' + noun 'пример' ('example/model'). It literally means 'I am not an example for you.' The dative marks the person for whom he is not an example.
segments.p2-6-g387: 'Я' is the pronoun subject 'I', here placed at the start for emphasis and followed by a pause (comma).
segments.p2-6-g388: 'может' is a colloquial short form of 'может быть' and expresses possibility or conjecture ('maybe,' 'might'); as an adverb/parenthetical it softens the assertion that follows.
segments.p2-6-g389: 'еще' here means 'still' / 'even' (intensifying the comparative); 'тебя' is the accusative form used colloquially in comparative phrases ('than you'); 'хуже' is the comparative 'worse.' Together they form the comparative clause '(be) even worse than you.'
segments.p2-6-g461: "Это" is the demonstrative pronoun introducing a nominal identification ('This is ...'). "безобразие" is a neuter noun meaning a moral outrage/disgrace; it is the predicate noun. "одно" follows the noun as a postpositive qualifier meaning 'mere/just' or 'simply' (lit. 'one'), adding emphatic limitation. The comma signals a pause linking to the following exclamation.
segments.p2-6-g462: "вот что" is a fixed exclamatory phrase; here "вот" functions as a pointing/explanatory particle and "что" is an exclamatory particle — together they intensify the preceding statement ('that's what it is!'). The short phrase is emphatic and stands apart intonationally.
segments.p2-6-g463: A single-word interjection. "Да" here is the ordinary affirmative 'yes' used as a quick, standalone reply; the dash in the narration marks direct speech.
segments.p2-6-g464: The adverbs "резко и поспешно" precede the subject to foreground the manner ('sharply and hastily'). "она" is the subject (feminine). "мне" is the dative object marking the person to whom the action was directed ('to me'). "поддакнула" is past tense, feminine singular of "поддакнуть" meaning 'to echo/assent' — a quick, echoed agreement; the past-tense form matches the feminine subject.
segments.p2-6-g465: "Меня" is fronted as the accusative personal pronoun ('me') and indicates the person affected; this fronting gives emphasis. "даже" is an adverb 'even', adding emphasis. "удивила" is past tense, third-person singular feminine and agrees with the following feminine noun "поспешность" (the real grammatical subject); the construction literally reads 'Me even surprised the hastiness...'. "этого да" is a genitive phrase modifying "поспешность" ('of that "yes"'), where "да" is the quoted affirmative utterance itself.
segments.p2-6-g450: сошлись is the past‑tense, plural form of the perfective reflexive verb сойтись. With no explicit subject it implies ‘we/they’ and can mean ‘met’, ‘came together’, or (here) ‘got together’ in the sense of beginning a relationship or becoming united.
segments.p2-6-g453: давеча is an adverb meaning ‘recently’ or ‘the other day’; it’s somewhat colloquial/dated and sets the time frame for the whole sentence.
segments.p2-6-g454: The clause literally says ‘and a word we the whole time to one another did not utter.’ слова (a word) + не молвили is the idiom ‘didn’t utter a word’; мы is the subject ‘we’; во все время (‘throughout the whole time’) is a temporal phrase modifying молвили; друг с дружкой is a reciprocal, old‑fashioned way to say ‘to one another’ (literally ‘one with the other’).
segments.p2-6-g455: и ты меня is a short clause fronting the person addressed: и = ‘and’, ты = subject ‘you’, меня = object ‘me’; the verb is postponed and understood in the following clause.
segments.p2-6-g456: как introduces a simile ‘like/as’; дикая is an adjective used substantively (‘a wild one’) agreeing in gender with the implied female addressee, so ‘like a wild creature’ or ‘like a savage.’
segments.p2-6-g457: уж потом (‘only/just afterwards’) is a temporal/intensive adverbial combination; рассматривать is the infinitive ‘to examine/inspect’; стала is past feminine of стать used with an infinitive to mean ‘began to’ — so стала рассматривать = ‘began to examine.’
segments.p2-6-g458: и я тебя также is elliptical: и = ‘and’, я = ‘I’, тебя = ‘you’ (object), также = ‘also/likewise’. The verb from the previous clause (began to examine) is understood, so this means ‘and I did the same to you.’
segments.p2-6-g459: Разве opens a surprised or rhetorical question (‘really?/is it so?’); эдак (colloquial) means ‘like this/in that way’; любят is the 3rd person plural present of любить, used here generically (‘people/they love’). Together this expresses incredulity: ‘Do people love like that?’
segments.p2-6-g460: Разве…эдак again frames a rhetorical/incredulous question. человек с человеком literally ‘person with person’ is a generic way to say ‘one person with another’ or ‘people with people’; сходиться is the infinitive ‘to come together/meet/get together’; должны is a plural predicate meaning ‘ought to / are supposed to’ — the structure asks whether people ought to get together in that way.
segments.p2-6-g401: А here is a discourse particle meaning 'but' or 'well' introducing a contrast; взять is an infinitive used idiomatically as a modal 'to suppose/assume' (common in Russian: 'взять, что...' = 'assume that...'); то adds demonstrative emphasis ('this/that') and helps form the set phrase 'взять то, что...'.
segments.p2-6-g402: что introduces a content clause ('that'); ты is the 2nd-person nominative subject; с + первого начала is a prepositional phrase meaning 'from the very beginning' (с = 'from/since', первого начала = 'the first beginning'); раба is an accusative form used as a blunt predicative noun after the dash, emphasizing 'a slave'.
segments.p2-6-g403: Да here is an interjection/exclamation roughly 'yes' or 'indeed', strengthening the following exclamation.
segments.p2-6-g404: раба is the accusative singular of раб used as an abrupt, contemptuous exclamation: 'a slave!' (the dash/exclamation gives force).
segments.p2-6-g405: Ты is the nominative subject; все here means 'everything' as the direct object; отдаешь is the 2nd-person singular present of отдавать used in the sense 'surrender/give up'. Word order subject–object–verb is normal and stresses 'you'.
segments.p2-6-g406: всю волю is an accusative noun phrase (всю = accusative feminine of весь modifying волю) meaning 'your entire will/freedom' and completes what is given away; the intensified adjective всю marks totality.
segments.p2-6-g407: И is the coordinating 'and' linking to the previous thought; порвать is an infinitive 'to tear/break' that pairs with the finite verb захочешь (you will want) in the clause structure 'порвать... захочешь' meaning 'you will want to tear...'; потом is 'afterwards/then'; эти цепи is the accusative object 'these chains'.
segments.p2-6-g408: да уж нет is a colloquial, emphatic negative formula: да here softens or introduces the retort, уж intensifies ('indeed/quite'), and нет is the blunt negation 'no' — together meaning 'but no' or 'not at all'.
segments.p2-6-g409: все крепче и крепче is a comparative repetition meaning 'increasingly/stronger and stronger' (все functions as an intensifier 'more and more'); будут is the 3rd-person plural future auxiliary paired with the imperfective опутывать to express an ongoing/frequentive future 'will be entangling'; тебя is the accusative object 'you'; опутывать is imperfective 'to entangle/ensnare'.
segments.p2-6-g491: Я (nominative pronoun) is the subject 'I.' Слышал is the past masculine singular of слышать, a simple past tense meaning 'heard' (here a completed/perceptual past). Ее is the possessive/accusative form 'her' modifying дыханье; глубокое is neuter singular adjective agreeing with дыханье (neuter); дыханье (older spelling of дыхание) is a neuter noun here in the direct object position meaning 'breathing' or 'breath.' Word order is Subject–Verb–Object.
segments.p2-6-g492: Зачем is an interrogative adverb 'why/what for.' Ты is the singular informal subject 'you.' Сюда is a directional adverb 'to here/hither.' Проехала is the past feminine singular form of the perfective verb проехать, so it asks about a completed movement by vehicle or carriage ('went/rode through' or more generally 'came here'); the feminine ending signals the speaker addresses a female or a grammatically feminine subject.
segments.p2-6-g493: Начал is past masculine singular of начать 'to begin,' placed before я for slight emphasis ('I began'). Уже is the adverb 'already' modifying the action. С is the preposition 'with' governing the instrumental. Некоторою is an older/archaic instrumental feminine form of некоторый (modern form некоторой), and властью is the instrumental of власть 'authority/power' — together 'with a certain authority.' The instrumental with с indicates accompaniment or manner.
segments.p2-6-g494: Так is an adverb meaning 'so/thus' (or an interjection-like response 'so,' 'that's it,' 'very well'). Here it functions as a brief confirming or concluding utterance in direct speech.
segments.p2-6-g410: Это here functions as a demonstrative/expletive subject ('this is/it's'). Уж is an emphatic particle intensifying the statement. Такая is the feminine singular demonstrative adjective agreeing with цепь; цепь is the feminine noun in nominative. Проклятая is a past-participle/adjectival modifier 'cursed' placed after the noun for a slightly colloquial/emphatic effect (Russian allows postpositive adjectives for force).
segments.p2-6-g411: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Ее is the accusative feminine object pronoun referring back to the feminine noun (the chain) — here 'it/her'. Знаю is 1st person singular present of знать meaning 'I know' (be familiar with/recognize).
segments.p2-6-g412: Уж is an emphatic particle introducing the clause. О другом is a prepositional phrase 'about something else' (другом is prepositional singular). Я и не говорю is 'I am not even speaking' — и here is emphatic ('even'), не negates, and говорю is present tense 'I say/speak'.
segments.p2-6-g413: Ты is the 2nd-person singular subject 'you'. И again functions as an intensifier ('even' / 'also'). Не поймёшь is the future indicative of понять (поймёшь) with negation: 'you will not understand' (here used idiomatically as 'you won't get it').
segments.p2-6-g414: Пожалуй is a parenthetical modal adverb meaning 'perhaps' / 'I suppose' used to soften or hedge the preceding statement.
segments.p2-6-g415: А is a contrastive conjunction 'but'. Вот is a discourse particle drawing attention ('now/well'). Скажи-ка is the imperative 'tell me' (скажи) with the colloquial particle -ка added to make the request less formal; the colon introduces the following question.
segments.p2-6-g416: Ведь is an emphatic particle meaning 'after all' or 'you know', and ты is the vocative/subject pronoun 'you' addressing the listener.
segments.p2-6-g417: Наверно is an adverb 'probably' or 'surely', used parenthetically to indicate the speaker's uncertainty or probability.
segments.p2-6-g418: Уж here is an intensifying particle (roughly 'surely' / 'already') preceding the predicate. Хозяйке is the dative singular of хозяйка — 'to the landlady/mistress' (marks the person to whom one is obliged). Должна is the short-form feminine predicate (from должный), used modally: 'you are obliged / you owe / you ought (to)' — its feminine form agrees with the implied ты (female addressee) or with a feminine grammatical subject.
segments.p2-6-g419: Ну is a discourse/interjectional particle often rendered 'well' — it introduces a reaction, conclusion, or mild impatience.
segments.p2-6-g420: Вот is a demonstrative/discourse particle ('there/now/well') drawing attention to the result. Видишь is 2nd person singular present of видеть but used idiomatically as 'you see!' meaning 'as you can see' or 'see, I told you'.
segments.p2-6-g390: Я is the nominative first-person singular pronoun 'I' and here stands at the sentence start followed by a comma for emphasis and a slight pause; it is the grammatical subject of the clauses that follow.
segments.p2-6-g391: впрочем is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' or 'besides' that softens or qualifies what comes next; it functions as a discourse connector and is set off by commas.
segments.p2-6-g392: пьяный (adjective) modifies the implied subject and is placed before the verb for emphasis; сюда is an adverb of place 'to here/hither'; зашел is the masculine singular past tense of зайти (perfective) 'entered/came in' — the word order (adjective + place + past verb) stresses the state (drunk) in which the entering occurred.
segments.p2-6-g393: поспешил is the masculine singular past of поспешить 'hastened' and here introduces an action: 'I hastened to...'; the pronoun я follows the verb for emphasis (common in Russian); все-таки is a fixed particle meaning 'nevertheless/after all'; оправдать себя is an infinitive phrase 'to justify/defend oneself' completing the verb поспешил.
segments.p2-6-g394: К тому ж is a fixed connective (к + dative тому + particle ж) meaning 'moreover' or 'besides'; мужчина is the nominative subject 'a man'; женщине is dative 'to a woman' and indicates the person for whom he would be a 'пример' (model); совсем + не gives a strengthened negation 'by no means/not at all'; the copula 'is' is omitted in the present tense, so пример is the nominal predicate.
segments.p2-6-g395: Дело is a neuter noun 'matter/affair' in the nominative; розное is an older/spelling variant of разное 'different' agreeing with дело — the clause states that the point under discussion is a different matter.
segments.p2-6-g396: я is the subject pronoun 'I'; хоть и forms a concessive conjunction 'though/even if' (хоть marks concession and и often accompanies it); гажу and мараю are present-tense 1st-person imperfective verbs ('I soil' / 'I tarnish') coordinated by и, with себя as the reflexive object — the present verbs express habitual or ongoing behavior despite the concession.
segments.p2-6-g397: да зато is a colloquial linking pair meaning 'but/and yet/on the other hand' (да acting as an older conjunction and зато 'on the other hand'); ничей is a possessive pronoun 'no one's' modifying the omitted noun; the word order ничей я не раб places я after ничей for emphasis; не раб is the nominal predicate 'not a slave' with no copula in present sense.
segments.p2-6-g398: был is the past tense (masculine) of быть 'was'; да here links clauses in a colloquial sequence ('was and'); пошел is the past tense of пойти 'set off/left' — together the short sequence means 'I was [here] and then went away'.
segments.p2-6-g399: и is a simple coordinating conjunction 'and'; нет is the existential negation 'there is no' (used with the genitive); меня is the genitive of я 'me' — нет меня literally 'there is no me' or idiomatically 'I'm not here/I'm gone'.
segments.p2-6-g400: Стряхнул is the past masculine singular perfective 'shook off' with an omitted subject (common in narrative; here understood as 'I'); с + себя ('с себя') means 'off myself' with с taking the genitive; и опять 'and again' links to the following predication; не тот is a predicate consisting of negation + demonstrative pronoun 'not that one/the same one' (copula omitted), meaning 'was not the same.'
segments.p2-6-g421: The dash marks reported speech; прибавил is past tense, perfective, masculine singular of прибавить and here means 'added' in the sense of adding a remark (speech). The subject pronoun я follows the verb (inverted for emphasis), a common word order in Russian narration.
segments.p2-6-g422: Хотя is a concessive conjunction 'although / though' introducing a subordinate clause. Она is the explicit subject, мне is the dative indirect object 'to me', не negates the past tense verb ответила 'did not answer' (perfective past).
segments.p2-6-g423: А is a contrasting conjunction 'but' linking to what follows; только is an adverb meaning 'only / merely'; молча is an adverb 'silently'. Together they mean 'but only silently'.
segments.p2-6-g424: Всем существом своим is an instrumental-phrase meaning 'with her whole being': всем (instrumental of весь) = 'whole/all', существом (instrumental of существо) = 'being/essence', and своим is the possessive pronoun agreeing in case/number/gender with существом. Слушала is past tense, feminine singular of слушать ('listened'), the verb whose subject (она) was given in the previous clause and so is omitted here.
segments.p2-6-g425: Вот тебе и цепь is an idiomatic exclamatory formula: вот is an interjection 'there/now', тебе is dative 'for/to you' (literal 'to you' in the idiom), и adds an emphatic 'and/even', and цепь is the noun 'chain' (here figurative). The construction 'вот тебе и X' means 'there you have X' or 'that's your X'.
segments.p2-6-g426: Уж is a colloquial/emphatic particle here (intensifier, roughly 'now/indeed'), никогда means 'never', не is the negative particle placed before the verb, and откупишься is future tense, second person singular, reflexive (откупиться) meaning 'to buy oneself off' or 'to redeem/buy one's way out'. The whole is a firm negative prediction about the addressee.
segments.p2-6-g427: Так is an adverb 'that way / thus', and сделают is future tense, third person plural of сделать 'they will do/make'. No subject is explicit; Russian allows omission because the verb form indicates 'they'. The sentence states what others will do.
segments.p2-6-g428: Все равно что is an idiomatic comparative phrase meaning 'it's all the same as / it's as though'. Все here contributes the sense 'completely / all the same', равно is 'equal/the same', and что introduces the comparison 'as/that'. Черту is dative of чёрт ('to the devil') and душу is accusative of душа ('the soul'), so the phrase literally reads 'the same as to (give) the soul to the devil' — i.e. 'as if giving one's soul to the devil.'
segments.p2-6-g434: И к тому же is a short, colloquial connective meaning “and besides” or “what's more”; here the particles are compacted as И (and) + к тому (to that/that) + ж (emphatic particle). The clause is elliptical: the pronoun я (I) stands alone as the subject, the rest of the predicate is understood from context.
segments.p2-6-g437: может быть is a fixed parenthetical expression meaning “maybe” or “perhaps”; грамматicaly it combines modal может (may/it may be) with the infinitive быть (to be) and functions as an adverbial/parenthetical, not as a full verb phrase here.
segments.p2-6-g438: тоже такой же is a stacked expression indicating sameness: тоже (also) + такой (such/that kind) + же (an emphatic particle attached to такой forming такой же = 'the same'). Несчастный is an adjective/noun in nominative describing the (implied) subject; together they mean “also the same kind of unhappy/miserable.”
segments.p2-6-g439: почем ты знаешь is a colloquial rhetorical question: почем (colloquial 'how/why/how come') + ты (you) + знаешь (you know). Word order places the interrogative adverb first for emphasis: “how do you know?”
segments.p2-6-g440: и нарочно в грязь лезу is a clause with a coordinating и (and) followed by нарочно (deliberately) and a motion/figurative verb phrase в грязь лезу (I get into the mud / I thrust myself into the dirt). В + accusative (грязь) indicates direction/into, and лезу is 1st person singular present of лезть (to climb / to thrust oneself in), used here in a figurative sense.
segments.p2-6-g441: тоже с тоски is a short causal phrase: тоже (also) + с (from) + тоски (genitive of тоска, 'melancholy' or 'longing'); с + genitive here gives the cause: “also from melancholy.”
segments.p2-6-g442: Ведь пьют же с горя: combines ведь (an emphatic particle meaning 'after all'/'you know') with a generic plural verb пьют (they drink) and the particle же for added emphasis; с горя (from grief) uses с + genitive to state cause. The clause comments generally on people’s behavior: 'after all, they drink from grief.'
segments.p2-6-g443: ну is an interjection/filler particle meaning 'well' or 'now' used to transition or prompt; here it introduces the speaker's personal contrast with the general statement.
segments.p2-6-g444: а вот я здесь - с горя is a contrastive clause: а (but/and) + вот (here/now, used to point to the speaker) + я (I) + здесь (here) with a dash leading to the causal phrase с горя (from grief). The dash marks an explanatory or emphatic pause: 'but here I am — from sorrow.'
segments.p2-6-g445: Ну скажи is an imperative prompt:ну (well/now) + скажи (imperative of сказать, 'say/tell'); it's a colloquial way to urge the listener to answer or acknowledge something.
segments.p2-6-g446: ну что тут хорошего: is a colloquial rhetorical question: ну (well) + что (what) + тут (here/at this point) + хорошего (genitive of хороший, 'good') — the genitive after что is common in expressions meaning 'what good is there' or 'what's good about it?'.
segments.p2-6-g447: вот мы с тобой is a compact colloquial phrase meaning 'here we are, you and I' or 'the two of us' — вот (here/now) + мы (we) + с (with) + тобой (you, instrumental). The instrumental construction 'мы с тобой' marks the accompaniment 'you and I together.'
segments.p2-6-g483: Более всего is an adverbial phrase meaning “above all/most of all.” Меня is the accusative object 'me' (placed before the subject for emphasis). Игра is the feminine noun 'the game/play' serving as the sentence subject, and увлекала is past tense, imperfective, indicative (feminine singular form agreeing with игра) meaning 'captivated/was carrying away'. The word order (adverbial → object → subject → verb) emphasizes the speaker as the one captivated.
segments.p2-6-g484: Она is the explicit feminine subject 'she.' Повернула is the past tense, perfective, transitive verb 'turned' (it takes a direct object). Свою голову is the reflexive possessive phrase 'her own head' in the accusative (object of повернула). Ближе ко мне combines the comparative adverb ближе 'closer' with the prepositional phrase ко мне 'to me' showing direction/position; и is the coordinating conjunction linking to the next parenthetical clause.
segments.p2-6-g485: Показалось мне is an impersonal construction meaning 'it seemed to me' (показалось is past tense; мне is dative, the experiencer). В темноте is a prepositional adverbial 'in the dark' specifying where the perception occurred. The verb is introductory/parenthetical to the following clause.
segments.p2-6-g486: Подперлась is past tense, reflexive/perfective (from подпереться) meaning 'propped/leaned (herself) against' and is feminine to agree with the subject. Рукой is the instrumental singular 'with her hand' indicating the means by which she propped herself (instrumental case marks 'with/by means of').
segments.p2-6-g487: Может быть is a fixed modal phrase meaning 'perhaps/perhaps it may be' (может here expresses possibility; быть is the infinitive used in the idiom). Together they function adverbially to qualify the following clause.
segments.p2-6-g488: Меня is the accusative object 'me.' Рассматривала is past tense, imperfective, feminine (implied subject 'she' from the previous sentence) meaning 'looked at/examined' or 'was looking at'—the imperfective aspect stresses the ongoing or background nature of the action.
segments.p2-6-g489: Как here is exclamatory 'how' (intensifier) introducing an exclamation. Жалел is past tense 'regretted'; the verb precedes the subject я for a more emotive, exclamatory word order. Я is the first-person subject 'I.'
segments.p2-6-g490: Что introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'. Не is negation modifying мог (past tense of мочь 'could/was able'), so 'не мог' = 'could not.' Разглядеть is the perfective infinitive 'to make out' and is the complement of мог (modal + infinitive). Ее is the possessive 'her,' and глаз is the direct object 'eye/eyes' in accusative (for inanimate nouns accusative equals nominative), so the clause means 'that I could not make out her eye(s).'
segments.p2-6-g466: "Значит" here is a discourse particle meaning "so/then/that means" that draws a conclusion from what came before; it does not function as a full verb in this position.
segments.p2-6-g467: «и у ней» literally "and at her"; the preposition у + pronoun (ней) indicates 'in/with her' or 'in her case', and the conjunction "и" here means "also"/"too" (adding her to whoever else was considered).
segments.p2-6-g468: «может быть» is the fixed modal phrase "perhaps/maybe" (может expressing possibility and быть the infinitive "to be"), marking uncertainty about the statement that follows.
segments.p2-6-g469: «та же самая мысль» is a stacked emphasis: the demonstrative "та" (that) + particle "же" (emphatic) + "самая" (the very) produce "that very same thought"; «бродила» is the imperfective past of "бродить" (to wander), so "was wandering/roaming"; «в голове» uses «в» + locative noun to mean "in (her) head/mind."
segments.p2-6-g470: A subordinate temporal clause introduced by «когда» (when) with past-tense verb «рассматривала» (she was examining/looking at me). «давеча» is colloquial for "recently/the other day," and «меня» is the accusative object ("me").
segments.p2-6-g471: As before, «Значит» functions as a discourse marker meaning "so/then/that means," introducing an inference from the prior remark.
segments.p2-6-g472: «и она уже способна к некоторым мыслям?» — «и» = "also/even"; «она» = "she"; «уже» = "already"; «способна» is the short-form adjective meaning "capable" and takes the preposition «к» + dative (here «некоторым мыслям") to mean "capable of certain thoughts." The phrase is a rhetorical question.
segments.p2-6-g475: «Черт возьми» is an idiomatic exclamation (literally "devil take [it]") used like "damn it" or "what the devil"; grammatically it's the noun "чёрт" plus imperative "возьми" (take), but here it's fixed as an interjection.
segments.p2-6-g476: «это любопытно» — «это» = "this/it"; «любопытно» is a predicate adverb meaning "curious/interesting/odd" (commenting on the situation rather than describing an inquisitive person).
segments.p2-6-g477: «это - сродни» = "this is akin to"; «сродни» is a short predicate meaning "akin to/akin" and links to the previous clause by repeating «это» for emphasis.
segments.p2-6-g478: «думал я» is the past-tense verb "I thought," with subject pronoun after the verb for emphasis or narrative tone (typical Russian word order allows verb before pronoun).
segments.p2-6-g479: «чуть не потирая себе руки» is a participial phrase (present active participle «потирая») modifying the subject of "думал я" — "almost rubbing my hands to myself"; «чуть не» + participle = "nearly/did almost [do it]."
segments.p2-6-g480: The whole line is a rhetorical question. «Да и как» is a colloquial connective meaning "and besides/how could..." ("Да" here works as a mild intensifier rather than a simple "yes"); «с молодой такой душой» uses «с» + instrumental ("with such a young soul"); «не справиться» is an infinitive in negative meaning "not to be able to cope/manage," producing the sense "how could one not cope with...".
segments.p2-6-g576: "А" here is a short exclamatory discourse particle (like "ah" or "well") used to catch attention or mark a small change of mind; it stands alone before the following phrase and does not change the sentence's grammar.
segments.p2-6-g577: "стало быть" is a fixed idiomatic connective meaning "so/therefore/it follows that"; grammatically it functions as a parenthetical discourse marker linking an inference to the prior context rather than as a literal compound of 'стать' + 'быть'.
segments.p2-6-g578: "слушает" is the present tense, 3rd person singular indicative of 'слушать' (to listen); the subject is omitted but understood from context ("he/she/it"), and the exclamation mark gives it an emphatic, observational tone — "(he) is listening!".
segments.p2-6-g579: "Так" here is a short discourse particle meaning "well/so/then" (a response marker). It introduces the speaker's reaction and does not act as a demonstrative adverb modifying a verb or phrase.
segments.p2-6-g580: "не знаю" is the first person singular present of 'знать' with negation: "I don't know." In Russian this phrase often functions as a formulaic, evasive, or self-effacing reply rather than a literal statement about knowledge.
segments.p2-6-g581: The single word is the addressee's name; placed after a comma it marks direct address (vocative-like function) — "Liza."
segments.p2-6-g582: "Видишь" is 2nd person singular present of "видеть" used idiomatically as an attention-getting or rhetorical 'you see' (i.e., 'you understand/consider this'); the colon marks that what follows is an illustrative example.
segments.p2-6-g583: "я знал одного отца" literally 'I knew one father'; "знал" is past imperfective 'knew' (indicating acquaintance or past familiarity), "одного" functions like 'a certain/one' and with an animate noun forces the object "отца" into the animate accusative (which here looks like the genitive form).
segments.p2-6-g584: "который был строгий" is a relative clause: "который" = 'who/which', "был" past of 'to be', and "строгий" is a predicate adjective in the nominative masculine singular agreeing with the antecedent ('a father was strict').
segments.p2-6-g585: The phrase "суровый человек" supplies another descriptive noun phrase ('a severe/harsh man'); both adjectives and the noun are in the nominative and complete the description started by the relative clause.
segments.p2-6-g586: "а" is a contrastive conjunction here ('and/yet/but'), "перед" is a preposition meaning 'before/in the presence of' and governs the following noun in the instrumental case ("дочерью"), "на коленках" ('on [his] knees') uses "на" + plural locative/insessive form to indicate posture, and "простаивал" is past imperfective indicating habitual or lingering action ('stood/lingered' or 'would stand/loiter') so the whole segment means he would kneel/stand there before his daughter.
segments.p2-6-g587: "руки-ноги ее" lists two direct objects 'hands and feet' with the possessive "ее" ('her') attached after the nouns (a natural word order in Russian), and "целовал" is past masculine singular 'he kissed'—together: 'he kissed her hands and feet.'
segments.p2-6-g588: "налюбоваться" is a perfective verb meaning 'to admire/satisfy one's admiration', here used with negation and the past-tense modal "не мог" ('could not') to form the idiom 'couldn't get enough of admiring'; note the infinitive preceding "не мог" is a stylistic word-order variation but means the same as "не мог налюбоваться".
segments.p2-6-g589: "право" at the end is a colloquial particle meaning 'really/truly' or 'indeed', used to strengthen the speaker's remark rather than the literal noun 'right'.
segments.p2-6-g590: Она is the personal pronoun 'she' (nominative). Танцует is present tense, 3rd-person singular of танцевать, meaning 'dances' or 'is dancing' (here performing at the event). На + вечере marks location/occasion ('at the evening/party'); вечере is prepositional case of вечер and here means 'an evening gathering' rather than just the time of day.
segments.p2-6-g591: А is a coordinating particle contrasting or continuing the narrative ('and/while/but'), он is the nominative 'he'. Стоит here is actually from стоять ('to stand') in present 3rd-person singular meaning 'stands'; note the provided lemma list shows стоить, so the correct lexical meaning is 'stands'. Пять часов is a duration ('for five hours') with часов in genitive after a numeral. На одном месте is a fixed locative phrase 'in/at one spot', where одном is prepositional/locative (в/на + prepositional) and месте is 'place/spot'.
segments.p2-6-g592: С нее глаз не спускает is an idiomatic verbless-looking phrase where the verb спускает (from спускать) with не marks 'does not take/let down'. С here means 'from/off' (motion away); нее is the pronoun 'her' in genitive; глаз is used singular to mean 'his eyes / gaze' in an idiomatic expression; не negates the verb; спускает literally 'lowers/releases' but together the phrase means 'doesn't take his eyes off (her)'.
segments.p2-6-g526: This is an emphatic interrogative where the two adjectives 'Какая' + 'такая' frame the speaker's incredulity: literally 'what (какая) such (такая) I girl'. Word order places the interrogative adjectives before the pronoun and noun for force; 'я' is the subject pronoun and 'девушка' the predicate noun. The construction is colloquial and expressive rather than a neutral question.
segments.p2-6-g527: 'прошептала' is the feminine past tense perfective form of 'прошептать' ('whispered'), placed before the pronoun ('она') for slight narrative emphasis. 'едва слышно' is an adverbial phrase: 'едва' = 'barely' modifies the predicative/impersonal 'слышно' ('audible'), giving the sense 'barely audible.'
segments.p2-6-g528: 'но' is the coordinating conjunction 'but' linking the clause; 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'расслышал' is the masculine past tense perfective of 'расслышать' meaning 'to make out' or 'to hear (clearly)'—here it signals the narrator succeeded in catching what was whispered.
segments.p2-6-g529: 'Черт возьми' is an idiomatic exclamation equivalent to 'Damn it' or 'Devil take it', where 'Черт' functions as a strong expletive and 'возьми' is the imperative of 'взять' used idiomatically. The two-word phrase is frozen as a colloquial interjection.
segments.p2-6-g530: Here 'да' is a colloquial particle adding emphasis/persistence (roughly 'but/indeed' in this context); 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'льщу' is the present tense first-person singular of 'льстить' (here given as 'льщу'), meaning 'I flatter' (i.e., 'I am flattering her'—the object is implied). The verb is imperfective, indicating the action/state of flattering.
segments.p2-6-g531: 'Это' is the neutral demonstrative pronoun 'this/that/it' serving as the subject; 'гадко' is a predicative adverb (from adjective 'гадкий') meaning 'nasty/disgusting' and functions as the comment about the situation. The short sentence is a blunt moral/judgmental statement.
segments.p2-6-g532: "А может," is a colloquial two-word discourse unit meaning 'perhaps' or 'maybe'; here А is a connective/contrastive particle that introduces the possibility and может is the verb indicating possibility rather than ability.
segments.p2-6-g533: И хорошо is a short clause meaning 'and (that is) good' or 'all the better'; и adds mild emphasis ('even/and'), and хорошо is an adjectival/adverbial predicate meaning 'good' or 'for the best' in this context.
segments.p2-6-g536: Она is the nominative pronoun 'she'. Молчала is the past imperfective form of молчать ('to be silent'), indicating she remained silent (ongoing or habitual past action); together they form a simple past statement.
segments.p2-6-g537: Видишь here is the present 2nd-person form of видеть used as a discourse marker 'you see' (not literal seeing), addressed to the listener to draw attention to what follows.
segments.p2-6-g538: Лиза is simply the vocative or direct address of the woman's name 'Liza' (written as the nominative form in Russian).
segments.p2-6-g539: Я про себя скажу is 'I will say (it) to myself.' Я is the subject 'I'; про себя is an adverbial phrase meaning 'to oneself' or 'to myself' (про + accusative pronoun), and скажу is the 1st-person singular future of сказать ('to say'), so the clause expresses a future, private remark.
segments.p2-6-g540: Была бы у меня семья с детства is a conditional clause: the past form Была plus the particle бы creates the subjunctive ('if there had been'); у меня marks possession ('I had' literally 'there would be at me'), семья is the possessed noun 'a family', and с детства ('since/from childhood') is a time phrase in the genitive.
segments.p2-6-g541: Не такой бы я был is the result clause: не negates такой ('such'/'so'), бы again marks the conditional, я is the subject, and был is the past form of 'to be'; word order (не такой бы я был rather than я бы не был такой) emphasizes 'not that kind of person'.
segments.p2-6-g542: Как теперь is a short comparative phrase 'as (I am) now'—как is the comparative 'as/like', and теперь means 'now'; the copula is omitted because it is supplied by был in the previous segment.
segments.p2-6-g497: This whole clause is an exclamatory phrase. 'А ведь' is a discourse particle meaning 'and yet/after all' that frames the thought; 'как' introduces an exclamation ('how'); 'хорошо' is an adverbial predicate meaning 'pleasant/nice'; 'в отцовском-то доме' is a prepositional phrase ('in the father's house') where '-то' is the emphatic particle attached to the adjective (отцовском) to stress that particular house; 'бы' is the conditional/wish particle making the idea hypothetical or wistful; 'жить' is the infinitive used as the main predicative ('to live'). Word order is typical for an exclamation ending in the infinitive, and the particles give the sentence its tentative, longing tone.
segments.p2-6-g498: 'Тепло' here is an exclamatory predicate/adverb meaning 'it is warm' or simply 'warm' as an isolated evaluative word; the subject is omitted and understood from context, so this functions like a one-word sentence giving the temperature/feeling of the place.
segments.p2-6-g499: 'привольно' is an adverb (from привольный) meaning 'free, carefree, at ease' or 'spaciously/comfortably'; the semicolon links it as a parallel descriptive comment to 'тепло', continuing the list of qualities of the place.
segments.p2-6-g500: This noun phrase 'гнездо своё' ('one's own nest') is elliptical (no verb) and completes the string of descriptors; 'гнездо' is a neuter noun 'nest' used metaphorically for a home, and 'своё' is the reflexive possessive pronoun agreeing with that neuter noun, meaning 'one's own'.
segments.p2-6-g501: 'А' is a contrastive particle ('and/but'); 'коль' is an archaic/poetic conditional conjunction meaning 'if' or 'suppose'; 'того' is the genitive demonstrative referring back to the preceding idea ('of that' / 'that being so'); 'хуже' is the comparative predicate 'worse'. Together they form a rhetorical question: 'And if that would make things worse?'
segments.p2-6-g502: 'В тон' is an idiom meaning 'into the tone' or 'the right tone/style'; 'надо попасть' is an impersonal modal construction ('one must hit' / 'you must hit'); 'попасть' is the infinitive following надо. The phrase is reported thought (hence the comma and opening quote) meaning 'you have to get the tone/right manner.'
segments.p2-6-g503: 'мелькнуло' is the neuter past form of мелькнуть used impersonally to report a fleeting idea ('it flashed/occurred'); 'во мне' ('in me') marks the locus of that fleeting thought. The dash and comma mark this as an interior reaction or passing thought.
segments.p2-6-g504: 'сантиментальностью' is the noun in instrumental case meaning 'by/with sentimentality' (denoting means or manner); the enclitic particle '-то' attached to the noun adds emphasis ('that very sentimentality' / 'with sentimentality, mind you'). Grammatically this phrase comments on the means or quality being criticized.
segments.p2-6-g505: 'пожалуй' is a hedge/mitigating particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', softening the following assertion; it commonly introduces a tentative judgment.
segments.p2-6-g506: This clause 'не много возьмёшь' literally 'you will not take much' is a colloquial, generic 2nd-person assertion meaning 'you won't get much' or 'it won't get you far'; 'не' negates 'много' ('not much') and 'возьмёшь' is 2nd person singular future indicative of взять used generically (English often renders it impersonally as 'one won't get much' or 'you won't get very far').
segments.p2-6-g550: "Я вот без семьи вырос;" is a compact clause: Я (I) is the subject, вот is a discourse particle adding immediacy or emphasis ('you see'/'you know'), без семьи is a prepositional phrase with без + genitive meaning 'without a family', and вырос is the perfective past verb (masculine singular) 'grew up'. Together it states 'I, you see, grew up without a family.'
segments.p2-6-g551: оттого is a conjunctive adverb meaning 'for that reason' or 'therefore' and links the preceding causal clause to the result stated after the comma.
segments.p2-6-g552: верно here is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'surely' or 'probably' (softening the causal claim); it modifies the speaker's assessment rather than the grammatical structure.
segments.p2-6-g553: такой и вышел: такой is a demonstrative/adjective 'such' (i.e. 'of that sort'), и is a small intensifying/connecting particle ('even/indeed' here), and вышел is past tense (perfective) of выйти used impersonally as 'turned out' or 'came out' (i.e. 'I turned out that way').
segments.p2-6-g556: бесчувственный is an adjective used predicatively and elliptically here (the full thought would be 'I am insensitive'). In this fragment it serves as a blunt characterization: 'insensitive' or 'unfeeling.'
segments.p2-6-g557: Я (I) is the subject; выждал is the past tense (perfective) of выждать meaning 'waited out' or 'bided my time' (here simply 'waited'); опять is the adverb 'again' placed after the verb for emphasis. The clause states 'I waited again' or 'I waited it out again.'
segments.p2-6-g558: Пожалуй is a modal/discourse particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', used to hedge or soften the following statement inside the quoted thought.
segments.p2-6-g559: и не понимает consists of и (a colloquial particle here, functioning like 'also' or an emphatic filler) + не (negation) + понимает (3rd person singular present of понимать 'to understand'); together 'and (she) doesn't understand' or 'she probably doesn't understand.'
segments.p2-6-g560: думал я is a past-tense phrase meaning 'I thought'; the verb думал (past masculine singular) precedes the pronoun for stylistic inversion, marking the remark as an internal thought.
segments.p2-6-g561: да и смешно - мораль: да и (an idiomatic combination meaning 'and besides' or 'moreover') + смешно ('ridiculous' or 'funny') is a comment, and the dash before мораль makes мораль ('the moral' or 'the lesson') the noun being evaluated — 'and besides, the moral is ridiculous.'
segments.p2-6-g543: Subject + prepositional object + adverb + verb in present tense: Я (nominative) is the 1st-person subject; об этом uses the preposition об with the neuter demonstrative это in the prepositional case (этом) meaning 'about that'; часто is an adverb modifying думаю; думаю is 1st-person singular present (imperfective) 'I think/reflect', indicating a repeated or habitual action. The clause is a simple declarative sentence and stands alone as the whole thought.
segments.p2-6-g544: Concessive clause using the fixed combination как бы ни + past (было): как introduces the clause, бы is the conditional/modal particle, and ни adds the concessive 'no matter how' sense; было is impersonal past tense of быть ('was' / 'might have been') and худо is a predicative adverb meaning 'badly' describing the state 'в семье' (prepositional case: 'in the family'). Весь/все before 'отец с матерью' is an adverbial/parenthetical particle here (всё/все-таки sense 'after all' / 'still') linking the concessive first half to the main clause which follows the dash.
segments.p2-6-g545: Coordinating contrast: а introduces a contrast 'but/and yet', followed by negation не and the plural noun враги in nominative meaning 'enemies'; this contrasts the preceding identification 'отец с матерью' by denying that those family members are enemies.
segments.p2-6-g546: Short adversative fragment continuing the negation: не + чужие, where чужие is plural nominative/short predicate meaning 'strangers/outsiders'; it reiterates that they are not strange to you. The sentence uses clipped phrases for emphasis rather than a full clause.
segments.p2-6-g547: Хоть here functions as 'at least' (offering a minimal concession). The time expression в год раз is an inverted, colloquial ordering corresponding to 'раз в год' ('once a year'); в + accusative/temporal noun год + раз ('time/occurrence'). Любовь is the direct object in accusative; тебе is the dative 'to you'; выкажут is 3rd-person plural future (perfective) of выказать 'to show/express', meaning 'they will show' or 'they will express'. The whole clause states a minimal, occasional demonstration of affection.
segments.p2-6-g548: Все-таки is a fixed adverbial phrase ('after all' / 'still'); here it is split into two parts in the text (Все таки). Ты знаешь is the main clause: ты (nominative) + знаешь (2nd-person singular present of знать) meaning 'you know' or 'you are aware'. The phrase functions to introduce the following content clause.
segments.p2-6-g549: Subordinate content clause introduced by что ('that'). Ты is the subject; у + себя is an idiomatic prepositional + reflexive construction meaning 'among your own / at your home / with people of your own' (себя in the genitive after у). The clause states what the listener knows: that they are 'with their own' rather than isolated.
segments.p2-6-g507: Впрочем is a sentence‑initial discourse particle meaning 'however'/'anyway' that softens or qualifies the previous statement; it stands alone and is marked off by a comma.
segments.p2-6-g508: Это is the neuter pronoun referring to the preceding idea ('that/it'); так is an adverb of manner ('like that'/'so'); только is an adverb meaning 'just'/'merely' that limits the action; мелькнуло is the perfective past neuter singular of мелькнуть, an intransitive verb used here for a fleeting perception or thought ('flashed through'). Grammatically the clause is a complete predication with 'это' as the neuter subject that governs the past‑tense verb.
segments.p2-6-g509: Клянусь is the 1st person singular present tense of клясться ('I swear') used as an emphatic parenthetical ('I swear,' 'honestly'). It functions as an interjection before the main clause.
segments.p2-6-g510: Она is the nominative subject 'she'; и here is an emphatic/augmentative particle ('indeed/even'), part of the set phrase и в самом деле meaning 'really/in fact'; в самом деле is an idiom ('in the very fact' → 'indeed/really') composed of preposition + short demonstrative + noun in locative/dative form; меня is the accusative object ('me') governed by интересовала, and интересовала is the imperfective past feminine singular of интересовать, agreeing with the feminine subject 'она' and meaning 'was interesting to' or 'interested' (note: интересовать takes the person who is interested in the accusative).
segments.p2-6-g511: К тому же is a fixed connective meaning 'besides'/'moreover' (К + тому + же combine to form the discourse link). Я был is the past tense ('I was'); как‑то is an adverbial particle ('somehow'/'somewhat') formed from как + то and softens or vagues the description; relaxed (расслаблен) and настроен are short predicative forms (passive/short adjectives/participial in function) describing the speaker's state—they follow the copula and agree implicitly with the subject. The coordinate и links the two predicative adjectives.
segments.p2-6-g512: Да и here is a colloquial discourse combination ('besides/and besides') where Да functions as a particle and и intensifies it; плутовство is a nominal abstract 'roguery'/'trickery' (subject); ведь is a modal particle meaning 'after all' that adds argumentive force; так легко modifies the verb and means 'so easily'; уживается is the present indicative singular of уживаться ('to get along'/'to coexist'), with плутовство as subject and чувством ('with feeling') in the instrumental case introduced by с. The clause states a general observation about compatibility.
segments.p2-6-g562: This is the protasis (the 'if' clause) introduced by the colloquial contracted conditional particle 'Если б' (= 'Если бы'). The verbs use past forms with the conditional particle ('я был', 'была б') to mark an unreal present: 'If I were a father and if I had a daughter of my own...'. Note the informal word order 'я был отец' (nominative) rather than the more formal 'я был бы отцом'; 'у меня' + 'была' expresses possession ('had').
segments.p2-6-g563: Short clause giving the conditional subject plus particle: 'я бы' = 'I would'. The particle 'бы/б' marks the conditional and here stands immediately after the pronoun for emphasis; the rest of the predicate follows later.
segments.p2-6-g564: 'Кажется' is a modal parenthetical in the present tense meaning 'it seems' or 'I suppose', hedging the statement; it doesn't affect the tense of the conditional construction but signals uncertainty or modesty.
segments.p2-6-g565: Here the object 'дочь' (daughter) and the comparative adverb 'больше' ('more') precede the verb — a normal Russian word order for emphasis: '(daughter) more (would) love' = 'would love the daughter more'.
segments.p2-6-g566: 'чем' introduces the standard comparative phrase 'больше, чем сыновей' = 'more than (my) sons'; 'сыновей' is genitive plural as required after 'чем' in comparisons.
segments.p2-6-g567: 'Любил' is the past-tense form of 'любить' used here in a conditional construction with 'я бы' to mean 'I would love'. In Russian the past tense plus 'бы' expresses what would happen hypothetically.
segments.p2-6-g568: The particle 'право' here is an interjection meaning something like 'really' or 'indeed', used to strengthen or authenticate the speaker's assertion; it's colloquial and not the noun 'right'.
segments.p2-6-g569: Verb-before-subject order 'начал я' is a stylistic inversion common in narration: 'I began'. 'Сбоку' functions adverbially as 'offhand/aside', indicating he started speaking as a side remark rather than addressing the main subject.
segments.p2-6-g570: The phrase 'точно не об том' means 'as if not about that' — 'точно' here has the sense 'as if' or 'as though' (not the precise sense 'exactly'), and 'об том' ('about that') uses the preposition 'об' + demonstrative pronoun 'том' to indicate topic.
segments.p2-6-g571: 'Чтоб' (short for 'чтобы') introduces a purpose/result infinitival clause: 'чтоб развлечь её' = 'to amuse her' or 'for the purpose of amusing her'. The infinitive 'развлечь' is the verbal nucleus and 'её' is the object pronoun 'her'. The whole phrase negated by the previous segment: 'as if not to amuse her.'
segments.p2-6-g572: Признаюсь is first-person singular (1st pers.) of признаться/признаваться used as a parenthetical interjection meaning 'I admit' or 'I must confess'; aspect-wise it can read as a present/future form but here functions as a discourse marker introducing an admission.
segments.p2-6-g573: я is the nominative pronoun 'I' (subject), and краснел is the past imperfective masculine singular of краснеть meaning 'to blush' or 'to redden'; imperfective past here conveys the occurrence or process in the past (English: 'I blushed' / 'I was blushing').
segments.p2-6-g574: "Это" is a demonstrative pronoun used here as 'this/that' and stands alone because Russian often omits a copula in present-tense questions; "зачем" is an interrogative adverb meaning 'for what/why', so together they form a short elliptical question equivalent to English "What is this for?/Why is this?".
segments.p2-6-g575: The verb "спросила" is past tense, perfective, feminine singular, indicating a single completed action ('asked'); placing the verb before "она" is a normal inverted word order in Russian narration. This short clause reports the speech act (direct speech tag) after the quoted question.
segments.p2-6-g513: An exclamatory interrogative phrase: 'Кто' is the interrogative pronoun 'who' and 'говорит' is present-tense 3rd person singular of 'говорить' ('to say/speak'). Together they form a short exclamation/retort—literally 'Who says (that)!'—used rhetorically.
segments.p2-6-g514: 'Поспешил' is the past tense, masculine singular, perfective form meaning 'hurried' or 'was quick to'; the subject pronoun 'я' follows the verb for slight emphasis. The infinitive 'ответить' ('to answer') completes the verb phrase 'поспешил ответить' = 'hastened to answer / answered too quickly.'
segments.p2-6-g515: A short impersonal clause: 'все' here means 'everything' or 'such things' and 'бывает' (present tense of 'бывать') means 'happens' or 'occurs' — idiomatically 'these things happen.'
segments.p2-6-g516: 'Я' is the subject 'I'; the particles 'ведь' and 'вот' are discourse markers adding emphasis/colloquial immediacy ('you see/after all/now'); 'уверен' is the short predicative adjective (masculine) meaning 'sure' or 'certain' — so the whole is 'I am, you see, certain.'
segments.p2-6-g517: 'Что' introduces a subordinate clause ('that'). 'Тебя' is the accusative object 'you' of the verb 'обидел'. 'Кто-нибудь' is the indefinite phrase 'someone/anyone' (кто + нибудь). 'Обидел' is past tense, perfective, meaning 'offended/wronged'. The coordinate phrase beginning with 'и' adds that 'скорей (more likely) перед тобой виноваты' — 'they are more likely guilty in front of you'; 'виноваты' is plural predicative agreeing with the implied plural subject (they), and 'перед тобой' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in your presence/in your eyes.'
segments.p2-6-g518: A comparative clause introduced by 'чем' ('than'). 'Ты' is the (implied) subject of the contrasted idea and 'перед ними' ('before them') completes the comparison; the verb is omitted (elliptical) because it repeats 'виноваты' from the previous clause.
segments.p2-6-g519: 'Я' is the subject 'I'; 'ведь' is a discourse particle ('you know/after all'); 'ничего' ('nothing') together with 'не знаю' (negative verb) gives 'I don't know anything'; 'из твоей истории' ('from/of your story') is a prepositional phrase specifying what is not known.
segments.p2-6-g520: A simple noun phrase: 'но' introduces contrast ('but'); 'такая' is the demonstrative/adjectival 'such/a...like that' modifying 'девушка' ('girl/young woman').
segments.p2-6-g521: 'Как ты' is a comparative clause meaning 'like you' or 'such as you' that specifies the kind of girl being referred to; word order is the standard comparative construction.
segments.p2-6-g522: 'Верно' is a sentence adverb here meaning 'surely' or 'certainly' used parenthetically to mark the speaker's confidence in the following statement.
segments.p2-6-g523: The clause negates an anticipated motive: 'не' negates the verb 'попадет' (future tense, 3rd person singular perfective 'will get/come'), 'с охоты своей' literally 'from her own desire' (with 'с' = 'from' and 'охоты' = 'desire'), and 'сюда' means 'to here/hither.' Together: '(she) will not come here from her own desire' = 'wouldn't have come here of her own accord.' The subject is omitted (understood 'she').
segments.p2-6-g612: Ревновал is the masculine past-tense form of the imperfective verb ревновать; with the particle бы it forms a conditional ('would be jealous'). The subject pronoun is omitted (typical in Russian), so the speaker means 'I would be jealous.' The leading dash marks direct speech.
segments.p2-6-g613: Ей-богу is an idiomatic, colloquial oath literally built from the dative forms 'ей' + 'богу' but used as an interjection meaning 'by God' or 'I swear to God'; it emphatically reinforces the preceding statement rather than functioning as a normal dative phrase.
segments.p2-6-g614: Ну is a discourse particle/interjection often translated 'well' and used to signal a reaction, mild impatience, or to introduce a rhetorical question.
segments.p2-6-g615: Как это is a colloquial interrogative phrase meaning 'how (on earth)/how is it that' where это functions as a filler pronoun; другого is the animate accusative (form identical to genitive) meaning 'another (person)' and serves as the direct object; она is the explicit subject 'she' placed after the object for emphasis (flexible Russian word order); станет + infinitive (станет целовать) uses the future auxiliary from стать meaning 'will begin to' or 'would begin to' and целовать is the imperfective infinitive 'to kiss' (implying ongoing or repeated action rather than a single perfective kiss).
segments.p2-6-g616: This is an elliptical, rhetorical question with the subject omitted (understood as 'she'). Чужого is animate accusative ('another/stranger' — here a person), больше is the comparative 'more,' and отца is in the genitive used after the comparative to mean 'than (her) father.' Любить is the infinitive 'to love' expressing the general action under question.
segments.p2-6-g617: Тяжело is an impersonal predicative adverb (from тяжёлый) meaning 'it is difficult'; это is a demonstrative pronoun referring to the preceding idea ('this/it'); и in this position functions as an emphatic particle meaning 'even' or 'also'; вообразить is the infinitive (perfective) 'to imagine' — together the clause says that imagining that situation is difficult or hardly conceivable.
segments.p2-6-g605: An impersonal exclamatory sentence built around the predicative adverb 'Весело' (it is pleasant/cheerful). 'иной девушке' is dative (for/ to some/another girl), so the whole phrase means 'to a certain girl it is pleasant'. 'дома' is an adverb meaning 'at home', and the infinitive 'жить' names the action ('to live'); together they form 'it is pleasant for some girls to live at home'.
segments.p2-6-g606: 'А' is a contrastive particle here ('as for' / 'but'); 'я' is the nominative pronoun 'I'. The particle 'бы' is the conditional marker that will attach to the past-tense verb later, marking a hypothetical ('I would ...').
segments.p2-6-g607: 'кажется' is a parenthetical verb meaning 'it seems' or 'I think' and qualifies the speaker's certainty about the following statement; punctuation (comma) sets it off as a hedge.
segments.p2-6-g608: 'свою дочь' is accusative ('my/one's own daughter'). 'и' here functions as an intensifier meaning 'even'/'also' in colloquial speech. 'замуж' is an adverbial/goal word used with verbs of giving away to indicate marriage ('to (into) a husband's home' → 'off in marriage'). 'не выдавал' is past tense imperfective of 'выдавать' with the conditional particle 'бы' understood from earlier, yielding 'would not give away / would not marry off' (imperfective used for the general/hypothetical sense).
segments.p2-6-g609: A brief colloquial question. 'Да' here is a small discourse particle (rather than literal 'yes') used to introduce or soften the question ('well'/'but'). 'как' is the interrogative 'how', and 'же' is an emphatic particle that intensifies the surprise of the question.
segments.p2-6-g610: 'спросила' is the past tense (perfective) verb 'asked'; the subject 'она' follows the verb (inverted order for narrative effect). This is a simple past narration: 'she asked.'
segments.p2-6-g611: 'чуть-чуть' is a reduplicated adverbial modifier meaning 'a little, slightly' (doubling adds colloquial soft emphasis). 'усмехнувшись' is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'having smirked/smiling wryly' and indicates the manner simultaneous with 'спросила' ('she asked, smiling slightly').
segments.p2-6-g662: This whole clause is a simple, general-statement sentence. И here is emphatic ('even' / 'also') rather than the plain coordinating 'and'. По + noun (бедности) functions idiomatically to mark the circumstance 'with respect to / in the state of poverty' (по is followed by the dative form here). Честные (adjective before noun) modifies люди; хорошо живут is present tense, 3rd-person plural, meaning 'they live/are living well' or 'they fare well' as a general truth.
segments.p2-6-g663: An interjection expressing hesitation or a thinking noise; functions like English 'hm' or 'hmm' and carries no grammatical role beyond that vocal reaction.
segments.p2-6-g666: A short affirmative particle meaning 'yes' here; note that da can also be an archaic conjunction ('and') or optative ('let'), but in this brief reply it is the simple affirmative.
