segments.p2-6-g667: A fixed modal phrase formed from 3rd-person present of мочь (может) plus the infinitive быть; together they function adverbially to mean 'perhaps/maybe'. Grammatically it's an impersonal/modal expression of possibility rather than a full clause with a subject.
segments.p2-6-g655: The adverb Подлинно (really/truly) modifies the adjective несчастная; несчастная is an adjective used substantively here ('the miserable one') and the personal pronoun ты follows it for emphasis (inverted word order for stronger reproach). Какая-нибудь is an indefinite exclamatory phrase ('some kind of' / 'what a…') that softens and intensifies the reproach; the whole line is an emphatic exclamation addressing the person as pitiable.
segments.p2-6-g656: Гм is an interjection expressing thoughtfulness or a dismissive grunt; it stands alone and has no grammatical role beyond conveying the speaker's reaction.
segments.p2-6-g659: Больше here means 'more' / 'mostly' (i.e., 'for the most part'); по + бедности uses по with the dative form бедности to indicate cause or circumstance ('because of/through poverty'). Всё это ('all this' or 'this whole set of things') is the subject phrase, and бывает is the habitual present tense of бывать meaning 'happens' or 'is (commonly) the case'.
segments.p2-6-g660: А introduces a contrast ('and/but'), while у + господ is a possession-style construction meaning 'as for the masters/gentry' or 'the masters have it ...'; то is a clitic particle adding emphasis/contrast after господ, and лучше is the comparative 'better' (here 'better off'). The clause is a contrastive statement preparing a rhetorical question tag.
segments.p2-6-g661: Что ль is a colloquial question tag (variant of что ли?) that asks for confirmation or expresses irony; что here functions as a particle in the tag and ль is the interrogative/enclitic particle that turns the preceding statement into a question.
segments.p2-6-g633: Это is a demonstrative pronoun used predicatively here (English 'that/it'); уж is a particle intensifying or confirming the statement ('indeed'/'certainly'); так is an adverb meaning 'so' or 'thus'. Together they form an elliptical copular sentence (no explicit verb 'is'), literally 'This indeed so.'
segments.p2-6-g634: Много ... бывает is an impersonal/predicative construction meaning 'much occurs' or 'there is a great deal'; из-за этого is the prepositional phrase 'because of this' (literally 'from/because of this') and в семьях is a locative phrase 'in families' specifying where; худа is a somewhat archaic/nominalized adjective meaning 'badness' or 'harm' used as the thing that 'happens'. Word order places the amount first, then cause, then location, then the predicate 'худа бывает'.
segments.p2-6-g635: Другие-то uses the contrast/emphatic particle -то attached to 'others' ('as for others'); the infinitive продать ('to sell') expresses the action that makes them glad; рады is the short-form plural adjective 'glad' (predicate); дочь is the direct object. The order (infinitive before predicate) is idiomatic: 'Others are glad to sell the daughter.'
segments.p2-6-g636: Не то что is a fixed contrasting phrase meaning 'not that (one)/far from' or 'not as if'; честью is the noun in the instrumental meaning 'with honour' or 'honourably' (manner), and отдать is the infinitive 'to give/hand over' (here 'to give a daughter away in honour'). The whole fragment contrasts what others would do with what they would not do 'in honour.'
segments.p2-6-g637: Проговорила is a past-tense, perfective verb (feminine) meaning 'uttered' or 'blurted out' — it marks a single completed act of speaking; она follows the verb for stylistic emphasis ('she'); вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly' modifying the utterance. Together: 'she suddenly said/blurtted out.'
segments.p2-6-g638: А here is a one-word interjection; although the particle 'а' commonly means 'but' or 'and', as an isolated exclamation it functions like English 'Ah!' or 'Oh!' expressing surprise or a sudden reaction.
segments.p2-6-g593: Помешался is past tense, masculine singular of the reflexive verb помешаться (here 'become obsessed' or 'go crazy about'); the -ся shows a change of state. На + ей (на ней) is the prepositional construction meaning 'about/over someone' (literally 'on her'), so Помешался на ней = 'became obsessed with her'.
segments.p2-6-g594: Я это понимаю is present-tense simple: я (I) + это (this/that, accusative demonstrative object) + понимаю (I understand). It states the speaker's current understanding of the previous clause.
segments.p2-6-g595: Устанет and заснет are perfective future forms (3rd person singular) of устать and заснуть: 'will get tired' and 'will fall asleep'. Ночью is an adverbial (at night). The dash links the two short future actions as a quick sequence.
segments.p2-6-g596: А introduces contrast ('and/but'); он is the subject 'he'. Проснётся and пойдёт are future perfective (3sg) 'will wake' and 'will go/set off'. Пойти + infinitive (пойдет ... целовать) expresses movement with purpose: 'go to kiss'. Сонную ее is accusative (сонную agrees with feminine ее) with the pronoun placed after the adjective for emphasis: 'her, sleepy' (i.e. 'to kiss her sleepy'). Крестить is another infinitive joined by и, here meaning 'to make the sign of the cross over' or to 'cross/bless'.
segments.p2-6-g597: Сам here is emphatic/reflexive meaning 'he himself' (emphasis on the subject). В + noun in the locative/prepositional (в сюртучишке) means 'in/inside a little tailcoat'—сюртучишке is a diminutive/shabby coat. Засаленном is an adjectival form agreeing in case and gender ('greasy/seedy'), and ходит is present-tense habitual 'he goes about/walks' (i.e. wears/goes around in it).
segments.p2-6-g598: Для всех uses the preposition для + genitive (всех) meaning 'toward/for everyone', and скупой is a predicate adjective in the nominative describing the subject omitted here (he is stingy with everyone). The phrase is an elliptical predication: 'he's stingy to everyone.'
segments.p2-6-g599: А introduces contrast ('but/while'); ей is dative 'for her'; из последнего literally 'out of the last [thing]' (последнего in genitive) idiomatically means 'out of his last (money/possessions)'. Покупает is present habitual 'he buys'—so 'but for her he even buys from his last.'
segments.p2-6-g600: Подарки (accusative plural) is the object 'gifts', дарит is present 'gives', and богатые is an adjective placed after the noun (inverted word order) meaning 'rich/costly'; together 'gives rich gifts'. The postpositive adjective is a stylistic inversion emphasizing the adjective.
segments.p2-6-g601: И уж радость ему is an idiomatic nominal predicate: и (and), уж (an intensifier 'already/indeed'), радость 'joy' as a noun functioning like 'it is a joy' with ему in the dative 'to him' — 'and already it is a joy to him'.
segments.p2-6-g602: Коль is an archaic/colloquial conditional meaning 'if'. Подарок is the grammatical subject 'the gift', and понравится is future perfective 3rd person singular of понравиться meaning 'will please' (i.e. 'if the gift pleases [her]'). The dative object (her) is omitted but recovered from the previous context (ей).
segments.p2-6-g603: Отец is the noun 'father' (nominative). Всегда is the adverb 'always'. Дочерей is genitive plural of дочь but here functions as the animate direct object (accusative plural of animate nouns equals genitive), 'daughters'. Больше modifies любит to form a comparative ('loves more'); любит is present 3rd-person singular of любить 'loves'. The word order (object before verb) places emphasis on 'daughters'.
segments.p2-6-g604: Чем introduces a comparative clause ('than'). Мать is nominative here and is understood as 'the mother (does)', the verb is omitted but supplied from the main clause (ellipsis common in Russian comparisons).
segments.p2-6-g618: Конечно is an adverbial discourse marker meaning 'of course' used to introduce or emphasize the statement; here it is a parenthetical comment and does not affect sentence morphology.
segments.p2-6-g619: Всё это is a nominal phrase ('all this') functioning as the subject; вздор is a predicate noun in a verbless (nominal) clause meaning 'nonsense' or 'rubbish' — Russian often drops the copula in the present tense.
segments.p2-6-g620: A repeat of the adverbial marker 'конечно' (same discourse use as the first occurrence), reinforcing the second clause; again parenthetical and emphatic.
segments.p2-6-g621: Всякий here means 'everyone' or 'each person' and is the subject; под конец is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning 'towards the end' or 'in the end' (под + noun used adverbially); образумится is perfective, reflexive, future tense ('will come to his senses' — 'образумиться' = to become sensible).
segments.p2-6-g622: Но is a coordinating conjunction 'but' introducing contrast; я is the personal pronoun 'I'; б is the clitic of the conditional ('бы'), here attached after the pronoun to mark a counterfactual or tentative 'would'.
segments.p2-6-g623: Кажется is an impersonal verb meaning 'it seems' or 'I think' (here parenthetical, hedging the assertion). It doesn't take a visible subject in this use.
segments.p2-6-g624: Прежде чем is a subordinating conjunction meaning 'before' (прежде + чем together introduce the subordinate clause); отдать is the infinitive 'to give/hand over' — here elliptical for 'to give her away (in marriage)'.
segments.p2-6-g625: Уж is an emphatic particle (here 'already' / 'indeed'); одной заботой uses the instrumental singular of забота ('by one care/just by worry') to indicate the means or cause; себя is the reflexive pronoun 'myself'; замучил is past tense, transitive perfective meaning 'would have worn (oneself) out' or 'tormented (oneself)'. The whole phrase means 'I would already have worn myself out just with worry.'
segments.p2-6-g626: Всех is genitive plural 'of all' (here 'all the suitors'); бы is the conditional particle again; женихов is the direct object in genitive plural 'suitors' (implying 'through/among all the suitors'); перебраковал is past conditional perfective meaning 'would have sorted out/weeded out' (figuratively: reject or cull the suitors).
segments.p2-6-g627: А is a contrasting conjunction ('and/yet' or 'but'); кончил is past tense of кончить 'to finish/end'; бы marks the conditional ('would have ended'); всё-таки is the adverbial particle (here split in the text as все таки) meaning 'after all' or 'nevertheless'; тем is the instrumental demonstrative 'by that' used with 'кончил' in the construction 'кончить тем, что...' = 'end with/by the fact that...'.
segments.p2-6-g628: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause; выдал is past tense of выдать and here uses the sense 'to give away (in marriage)' (выдать кого-то за кого-то = 'to marry off to'); бы is the conditional particle; за + того means 'to/for that one' — за here marks the person to whom she would be given (i.e., 'marry off to that one').
segments.p2-6-g629: Кого is the object relative pronoun 'whom'; она is 'she'; сама is a reflexive/emphatic pronoun 'herself' (emphasizing that it is she, personally, who loves him); любит is present tense 'loves' (simple present, general truth about her feelings).
segments.p2-6-g630: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning 'after all' or 'you see' that strengthens the following statement; тот is the demonstrative 'that one' or 'the one' and serves as the head of the noun phrase to be specified by the relative clause.
segments.p2-6-g631: Кого begins the relative clause ('whom'); дочь is the subject 'the daughter'; сама is emphatic 'herself' (stressing that it is the daughter personally who loves); полюбит is perfective future (polюбить) meaning 'will fall in love with' or, in generalizing contexts, 'falls in love with' — here used to describe the man whom she loves.
segments.p2-6-g632: Всегда is an adverb 'always'; всех is the genitive plural 'of all' used in the comparative construction 'хуже всех' = 'worse than all (others)'; хуже is the comparative of 'плохой' meaning 'worse' (comparative degree); отцу is dative 'to the father' — кажется takes a dative experiencer here, so кажется means 'seems to (the father)'.
segments.p2-6-g695: В первое-то время: a temporal phrase meaning 'at first / in the very first period'; the particle -то attached to первое gives an emphatic/pointing tone. даже и: the double particle 'даже и' intensifies to mean 'even'. ссоры с мужем: 'ссоры' is the plural object 'quarrels' and 'с мужем' uses the instrumental to mean 'with (her) husband.' хорошо кончаются: 'кончаются' is 3rd person plural present ('end, finish') with the adverb 'хорошо' placed before it.
segments.p2-6-g696: Иная сама: 'Иная' (feminine) here means 'one woman / a certain woman' and 'сама' is an emphatic reflexive used to stress 'she herself'. чем больше любит: a comparative clause 'the more (she) loves' introduced by 'чем' (standard particle in the correlative 'чем... тем...').
segments.p2-6-g697: тем больше ... заваривает: 'тем' completes the correlative ('the more ... the more ...') and here governs the comparative result. 'ссоры с мужем' is the (accusative) object 'quarrels with her husband' and 'заваривает' is present 3rd-singular of 'заваривать' used colloquially to mean 'stirs up / causes' (not literally 'brews').
segments.p2-6-g698: Право: an interjection meaning 'indeed/really' used to emphasize the speaker's remark.
segments.p2-6-g699: я знал такую: 'I knew such (a one):' — 'такую' (feminine) refers back to 'иная' (a particular woman); the colon introduces her quoted words.
segments.p2-6-g700: "Так вот,": a colloquial discourse marker combining 'так' and 'вот' meaning roughly 'well, then' or 'so, well' at the start of reported speech.
segments.p2-6-g701: люблю,: first-person present 'I love' used inside the quoted speech; it's a simple present indicative.
segments.p2-6-g702: дескать,: a parenthetical particle marking reported or ironized speech — 'she says/so she claims' (often glossed 'as if' or 'she says').
segments.p2-6-g703: очень и из любви тебя мучаю,: 'очень' ('very') intensifies; 'и' here adds emphasis ('and/even'); 'из любви' ('out of love') gives the motive; 'тебя мучаю' is 1st person present 'I torment you' with 'тебя' as direct object — all inside the quoted speech as the woman's supposed words.
segments.p2-6-g704: а ты чувствуй": 'а' introduces a contrastive or directive 'and/as for you'; 'ты' is the vocative/subject 'you'; 'чувствуй' is the imperative 'feel (it)' — the woman telling him to 'feel' the torment she claims is out of love.
segments.p2-6-g705: Знаешь ли,: 'Знаешь' is 2nd person singular present 'do you know'; the enclitic 'ли' turns it into a yes/no question or rhetorical question ('do you know whether / do you know if').
segments.p2-6-g706: что из любви нарочно человека можно мучить?: 'что' introduces the content clause ('that ...'); 'из любви' = 'out of love' (motive); 'нарочно' = 'deliberately/on purpose'; 'человека' is the accusative object 'a person'; 'можно' is the impersonal/modal 'it is possible / one can'; and 'мучить' is the infinitive 'to torment' — together forming 'that out of love one can deliberately torment a person.'
segments.p2-6-g707: 'Все больше' here is an idiomatic intensifier meaning 'all the more' or 'even more so' (in modern orthography it would be всё больше). The short sentence is elliptical: the adverbial phrase 'всё больше' precedes the noun 'женщины' in the nominative to indicate which group this intensification applies to — 'women, even more so.'
segments.p2-6-g708: А is a contrastive conjunction ('and/but') linking to the previous thought. 'Сама' is emphatic ('herself'), and 'про себя' is an idiom meaning 'to herself' (thinking inwardly). 'Думает' is present tense, 3rd person singular, indicating her current thought.
segments.p2-6-g709: This begins the quoted first-person thought. 'Зато' introduces a compensatory or contrasting idea ('on the other hand' / 'but'), 'уж' is an intensifying particle ('indeed' / 'already' in colloquial force), 'так' modifies the manner ('so/that much'), and 'буду' is the auxiliary 1st-person future of 'быть' used with the imperfective infinitive 'любить' to form 'I will love' (ongoing/future habit). 'Потом' places this action 'later.' Word order places the future auxiliary before the infinitive as in standard Russian.
segments.p2-6-g710: 'Так' again intensifies the manner ('so'), and 'заласкаю' is a perfective 1st-person singular future verb ('I will caress / pamper'). Together they continue the speaker's promise of exaggerated future tenderness.
segments.p2-6-g711: Частица 'что' introduces a result clause ('so ... that ...'). 'Не грех' is an impersonal expression meaning 'it is no sin' or 'it would not be sinful.' 'Теперь' is a temporal adverb 'now,' and 'и' here functions as an intensifier meaning 'even.' 'Помучить' is the perfective infinitive 'to torment (briefly),' completing the result clause: 'so that now it wouldn't even be a sin to torment (him).'
segments.p2-6-g712: И is a simple coordinating conjunction ('and/also'). 'В доме' is a locative prepositional phrase ('in the house'). 'Все' here means 'everyone' (nominative plural). 'На вас радуются' uses the verb 'радоваться' with the preposition 'на' + accusative to indicate being glad about someone ('rejoice for/about you'); 'радуются' is present tense, 3rd person plural.
segments.p2-6-g713: A series of short predicate comments follows, coordinated by repeated 'и'. 'Хорошо' is a predicate/adverb meaning 'good' or 'well' — a brief evaluative remark about the situation.
segments.p2-6-g714: 'И весело' continues the list with 'весело' as an adverb/predicate meaning 'merry' or 'cheerful.' The repeated 'и' simply strings these qualities together.
segments.p2-6-g715: 'И мирно' adds 'peacefully' ('мирно') to the enumerated qualities; again an adjectival/adverbial predicate describing the atmosphere.
segments.p2-6-g716: 'И честно' closes the list with 'честно' meaning 'honestly' or 'fairly,' completing the speaker's picture of a wholesome household atmosphere.
segments.p2-6-g719: Вот is a particle used to draw attention ('look' / 'well'), introducing the observation. 'Другие' means 'others,' 'тоже' = 'also/too.' 'Ревнивы' is an adjectival predicate 'jealous.' 'Бывают' is the verb used here in a general/habitual sense ('are sometimes' / 'turn out to be' / 'can be'), giving a nuance of occasional or typical occurrence rather than a single factual present action.
segments.p2-6-g639: "Вон оно что!" is a compact exclamatory formula meaning roughly "so that's what it is" or "ah, so that's the case." Grammatically, "вон" functions as a demonstrative/interjective particle drawing attention (here: "look/so"), "оно" is the neuter pronoun referring to the matter just understood, and "что" completes the exclamation ("what/that which it is"). The whole string is colloquial and idiomatic rather than a literal clause.
segments.p2-6-g640: "Это" here is a demonstrative pronoun used to introduce or point to the statement that follows (like "this/that (is)"). It serves as a discourse opener before addressing Liza.
segments.p2-6-g641: A direct address: the proper name stands in vocative function (comma after it). No grammatical change to the sentence structure — it simply names the interlocutor.
segments.p2-6-g642: Prepositional phrase of place: "в" + prepositional plural "тех семьях" ("in those families"). The adjective "проклятых" follows the noun for emphasis (postpositive) giving "in those accursed families."
segments.p2-6-g643: "Где" introduces a relative clause meaning "where." "Ни" before a noun is the emphatic negative particle meaning "not even" or as part of the neither/nor pair; here it governs the genitive "бога" (common after negation/partitive sense).
segments.p2-6-g644: Continuation of the coordinate negative: "ни любви не бывает" — "nor (is there) love". "Ни ... ни ... не бывает" is a pattern that stacks negatives ("neither ... nor ... exists"); "бывает" is used in the impersonal/habitual sense "to be/happen".
segments.p2-6-g645: "С жаром" (instrumental) literally "with heat," idiomatically "with ardor/vehemence." "Подхватил я" is past tense, perfective; here "подхватил" means "I took up" (a theme/remark) and the subject pronoun "я" follows the verb for emphatic, colloquial word order.
segments.p2-6-g646: "А" is a contrasting conjunction here ("and/but/whereas"). The clause repeats the pattern "где любви не бывает" — "where love does not exist" — using "бывает" again in the impersonal/habitual sense.
segments.p2-6-g647: "Там и рассудка не бывает." — "there even reason does not exist." "Там" (= "in such places") points back to the families; particle "и" here has an emphatic value ("even/also"); "рассудка" is the genitive of "рассудок" used existentially with "не бывает" to mean "there is no reason/sanity."
segments.p2-6-g648: "Такие есть семьи" is an existential statement: "Такие" (plural demonstrative "such") plus existential verb "есть" gives "There are such families." "Семьи" is plural (nominative) as the subject/complement of the existential construction.
segments.p2-6-g649: Parenthetical "правда" here is a modal/colloquial tag meaning "indeed/that's true" (used to concede or soften the previous statement). It does not change the clause's core grammar.
segments.p2-6-g650: "Да" in colloquial speech can function like a weak conjunction "but/and/anyway" introducing the contrasting comment "я не об них говорю" (I am not speaking about them). "Не об них говорю" is present-tense "I am not speaking about them," where "об" governs the pronoun "них."
segments.p2-6-g651: Direct address: the pronoun "Ты" (you, singular) names the addressee and is set off with commas in the sentence.
segments.p2-6-g652: "Видно" is an adverb meaning "apparently/it seems" — a parenthetical comment indicating inference by the speaker, not a full clause predicate.
segments.p2-6-g653: Prepositional phrase "в своей семье" ("in your own family") uses the reflexive possessive "своей" agreeing in gender/case with "семье" (prepositional singular). "Не видала" is past feminine singular of colloquial/archaic verb "видать" meaning "didn't see/experience," and "добра" is the genitive (partitive) of "добро" meaning "good/things good" — together "didn't see any good."
segments.p2-6-g654: "Что так говоришь." — here "что" is a conjunction meaning "that/which" linking the reason clause to the previous clause; "так" means "so/that way," and "говоришь" is present 2nd-person "you say/speak." The clause expresses cause: "which is why you say that."
segments.p2-6-g668: Опять here is a discourse marker meaning 'besides/again' introducing an additional point; и то is a colloquial connective that reinforces that move (literally 'and that'), so the whole short phrase flags a new remark before addressing Liza.
segments.p2-6-g669: Лиза: is the vocative form (a name followed by a colon) addressing the listener; no grammatical change is required — it's simply direct address.
segments.p2-6-g670: человек is used generically ('a person'); только is an adverb meaning 'only/merely' modifying what follows; свое is the reflexive possessive 'one's own' agreeing with человек; горе is a noun 'grief/misfortune'; любит + infinitive (считать) here expresses a habitual inclination ('likes to/counts on doing X').
segments.p2-6-g671: а introduces contrast ('but'); счастья своего is a genitive phrase 'his own happiness' (счастья is genitive of счастье, свое/своего agrees); не modifies the verb считать making it negative — 'does not count/notice.'
segments.p2-6-g672: А is a contrastive conjunction ('but'); счел is the perfective past of счесть used here in a conditional counterfactual meaning ('had he counted/considered'); бы is the conditional particle marking the unreal past; как должно is an adverbial phrase meaning 'as is proper / properly' (как + adjective in neuter form долж­но).
segments.p2-6-g673: так (in this construction) means 'then' / 'in that case'; the particle и here is emphatic (roughly 'indeed'/'actually'); увидел is past-perfective 'would have seen' and, together with бы, forms the polite/unreal conditional 'would have seen.'
segments.p2-6-g674: что introduces a subordinate clause 'that ...'; на + accusative here has a purposive/benefactive sense ('for'/'intended for'); всякую долю ('every/each share/portion') uses всякую as an adjective modifying долю; его is a possessive pronoun 'his'; запасено is a short passive/mediate perfect-past participle meaning 'is reserved/has been put aside' (impersonal passive construction).
segments.p2-6-g675: Ну is an interjection 'well' used to preface a colloquial hypothetical remark.
segments.p2-6-g676: а что is a conversational phrase 'and what (if)...' used to introduce a hypothetical or rhetorical scenario.
segments.p2-6-g677: коли is an archaic/colloquial conjunction meaning 'if' or 'supposing'; в семье is a prepositional phrase 'in the family'; все is 'everything' (subject of удастся); удастся is perfective, impersonal/reflexive meaning 'will turn out well / will succeed' (literally 'will be achieved').
segments.p2-6-g678: бог is the noun 'God'; благословит is future-tense perfective 'will bless' or an interjectional invocation 'God bless it' used parenthetically to wish good fortune.
segments.p2-6-g679: муж here means 'husband' (the man one marries); выйдет is perfective future of выйти used idiomatically with adjectives to mean 'will turn out / will prove to be' (выйдет хороший = 'will turn out to be a good man'); хороший is the adjective 'good.'
segments.p2-6-g680: любит тебя is a simple present indicative 'loves you' used here to paint the expected habitual behavior of the husband.
segments.p2-6-g681: лелеет тебя is another present-tense clause 'cherishes you' (лелеять = to cherish/cherish tenderly), repeating the pattern of expected behavior.
segments.p2-6-g682: не negates the verb; отходит here means 'to leave / to step away' (so не отходит от тебя = 'does not leave you'); от is the preposition 'from' and тебя is the object pronoun 'you.'
segments.p2-6-g683: Хорошо is an impersonal predicate meaning 'good/well' or 'pleasant'. В + той семье is the locative expression 'in that family' (той is the feminine singular demonstrative in prepositional case; семье is prepositional of семья). The whole exclamation evaluates the situation or atmosphere inside that household.
segments.p2-6-g794: Кто is the nominative interrogative pronoun 'who' serving as the subject. Это here is a demonstrative/discourse particle referring back to the preceding statement (more like 'that' or 'it'), placed between subject and verb for emphasis. Говорит is present tense, 3rd person singular imperfective of говорить, used here as a generic or rhetorical 'says' (or 'is saying').
segments.p2-6-g795: Это is a demonstrative pronoun functioning as the subject ('this/it'). Счастье is a neuter noun in the nominative singular serving as the predicative noun. Небесное is an adjectival predicate (neuter nominative singular) modifying счастье; the phrase is an emphatic nominal exclamation.
segments.p2-6-g796: Любишь is present tense, 2nd person singular indicative (imperfective) of любить, here forming a direct question. Ты is the explicit 2nd-person singular pronoun, included for familiarity or emphasis. Маленьких детей is the direct object ‘little children’; because children are animate, the plural accusative takes the genitive form (детей) and the adjective маленьких is in genitive plural to agree.
segments.p2-6-g797: Лиза is a proper name in the nominative used here in direct address (vocative function in English), separated by a comma in speech to call or address the listener.
segments.p2-6-g798: Я is the nominative first-person pronoun 'I'. Ужасно is an adverb of degree used as an intensifier here ('terribly'/'very'), modifying the verb. Люблю is first-person singular present indicative of любить; the direct object is omitted because it is understood from the previous question (i.e., 'children').
segments.p2-6-g684: 'Даже' is an emphatic particle meaning "even"; 'иной раз' is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning "at times/sometimes" (literally "another time"). The sequence 'и с горем пополам' contains the idiom 'с горем пополам' (literally "with grief half-and-half") meaning "with great difficulty/just barely," where 'и' adds an extra concessive/confirmatory shade ("even/also"). 'Хорошо' is the short predicate meaning "it's all right/good." The whole clause asserts that even with hardship things can be fine.
segments.p2-6-g685: The pair 'да и' acts as a colloquial connective meaning "and/besides/after all." 'Где' is the interrogative "where," and 'горя' is the genitive of 'горе' because 'нет' (existential negation) requires genitive; together 'где горя нет?' is a rhetorical question "where is there no grief?" with 'нет' denying existence.
segments.p2-6-g686: 'Выйдешь' is 2nd person singular future (perfective) of 'выйти.' Here it begins the expression 'выйдешь ... замуж' (see next segment) — together they form the idiom 'выйти замуж' meaning "to get married" (so the verb is future, addressing 'you').
segments.p2-6-g687: 'может' is a parenthetical modal adverb here meaning "maybe/perhaps" (from 'мочь' used as possibility marker); it interrupts the sentence and weakens the assertion that follows.
segments.p2-6-g688: 'замуж' is the adverbial/complement used with 'выйти' to form the collocation 'выйти замуж' = "to marry" (specifically said of a woman). It completes the action introduced by 'выйдешь.'
segments.p2-6-g689: 'Сама' is an emphatic/reflexive pronoun meaning "yourself" (feminine); it stresses that the subject personally will learn. 'Узнаешь' is 2nd person singular future of 'узнать/узнавать' meaning "you will find out/learn." Together they form the clause "you'll find out yourself."
segments.p2-6-g690: Зато is a discourse particle meaning 'on the other hand' or 'but' that introduces a contrasting example. Взять here is an informal infinitive used like English 'take' or 'suppose' (i.e. 'assume, for instance'), not a full past action; хоть ... в первое-то время is an adverbial time phrase 'at least in the first period' (хоть = 'at least/even', в = 'in/during', первое-то = 'the very first' with то as an emphatic particle). Замужем is a predicative/state word 'to be married' and за тем begins the prepositional idea 'to/with that one' (за + demonstrative), so the whole phrase proposes the hypothetical of being married, at least initially, to that person.
segments.p2-6-g691: Кого is the accusative interrogative 'whom' functioning as the direct object of любишь; любишь is 2nd person singular present indicative of любить ('you love'), here used rhetorically (addressing 'you' in general).
segments.p2-6-g692: Счастья is the genitive of счастие used exclamatorily ('happiness!') and то is the emphatic particle attached to it (roughly '— what happiness!'). The comma marks the pause of exclamation.
segments.p2-6-g693: The phrase repeats счастья-то for emphasis followed by the exclamatory сколько 'how much' (implied in the segment text) and the time phrase иной раз 'sometimes' (иной = 'another/other' used in the idiom иной раз = 'at times, sometimes'). Придет is the future (3rd person singular) of прийти 'to come' — here meaning 'will come/arrive/occur' (i.e. 'how much happiness will sometimes come').
segments.p2-6-g694: This is a compact colloquial cluster: the initial да и is a conversational 'and besides/and also', сплошь is an adverb 'entirely/throughout', the second да is another connective/emphatic 'and', and рядом means 'near/by one another' or 'close together'. Together it conveys an idiomatic 'and moreover it's continuous and all clustered together'.
segments.p2-6-g781: Дети is the nominative plural subject ('children'). растут is the 3rd-person plural present indicative of расти, meaning 'are growing' / 'grow up'; the verb follows the subject in plain declarative word order and the comma marks a pause before the next clause.
segments.p2-6-g782: чувствуешь is 2nd-person singular present indicative of чувствовать, used here parenthetically as 'you feel' or 'you sense' — it addresses the listener/reader directly and introduces the subordinate content clauses that follow.
segments.p2-6-g783: что is a subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing a content clause; ты is the nominative pronoun 'you' (subject); им is the dative plural 'to them' marking the beneficiary; пример is a nominal predicate ('example') with the copula omitted, so the structure means 'that you are an example to them'.
segments.p2-6-g784: The second что again introduces a parallel predicate; ты (you) + им (to them) + поддержка (noun predicate 'support') — Russian omits the present copula, so this reads 'that you are their support'. The semicolon separates this cluster from the next clause.
segments.p2-6-g785: что is 'that' introducing another clause; и here functions as an emphatic particle 'even' (not just coordinate 'and'); умрешь is 2nd-person singular future of умереть ('you will die' / 'you die'), and ты is postposed for emphasis: 'that even you will die.'
segments.p2-6-g786: они ('they') is the subject; всю жизнь ('all [their] life') is an adverbial temporal phrase placed before the verb; чувства и мысли твои places the nouns before the possessive (твои after the nouns for emphasis) — 'your feelings and thoughts'; будут носить is the future periphrasis (будут + infinitive) 'will carry/wear'; на себе is a reflexive locative 'on themselves' meaning 'carry with them/ bear on themselves.'
segments.p2-6-g787: так как is a causal conjunction 'since/because' (the parts are listed separately: так + как); от тебя ('from you' with the preposition от plus genitive тебя) is the agent/source; получили is past plural 'received' agreeing with они — 'because they received from you.'
segments.p2-6-g788: твой (your) modifies образ (image) and подобие (likeness); the verb примут is 3rd-person plural future of принять and here means 'will take on / adopt' (they will assume your image and likeness). Word order places the possessive before the nouns for normal attribution.
segments.p2-6-g789: Значит functions as a discourse marker 'so/therefore/that means' used to draw a conclusion from what came before; it's not a full verbal predicate here but a connective particle.
segments.p2-6-g790: Это is the demonstrative/subject 'this/it', великий is the attributive adjective 'great', and долг is a nominative noun meaning 'duty/obligation' — together forming the straightforward predication 'this is a great duty.'
segments.p2-6-g791: Как тут не сойтись тесней — a rhetorical question built on the infinitive сойтись with negation (не), meaning 'to come together / grow closer'. Как тут functions as 'how, in such circumstances', with тут 'at this point / here' limiting the context. Тесней is the comparative adverb (from тесный) modifying сойтись; отцу (dative) together with с матерью (preposition с + instrumental) names the two participants — 'the father with the mother' — so the phrase asks how they could fail to come/become closer.
segments.p2-6-g792: Говорят вот — an impersonal, colloquial opening: говорить in 3rd person plural 'they say / people say'. Вот is a discourse particle here ('you know' / 'well') adding emphasis or drawing attention to the remark.
segments.p2-6-g793: детей иметь тяжело? — an impersonal infinitival clause used as a predicate: иметь детей 'to have children' with детей in the object form (animate plural) and тяжело as a predicative adverb meaning 'is burdensome / difficult'. The whole is a rhetorical question asking whether having children is difficult.
segments.p2-6-g758: Неужто is an emphatic particle expressing surprise or incredulity ('could it be that...'). Нельзя is an impersonal modal predicate meaning 'it is not possible / one cannot' (no grammatical subject). Ее is the unstressed object pronoun 'her' placed before the infinitive (normal in Russian). Поддержать is a perfective infinitive 'to support/sustain' and completes the impersonal construction (what cannot be done is 'to support her').
segments.p2-6-g759: Редко ('rarely') here begins an elliptical impersonal clause; the full idea is 'Редко бывает такой случай' ('rarely does such a case occur'). Такой modifies случай and together mean 'such a case' or 'a case like that'. Word order (adverb + demonstrative + noun) is idiomatic for the speaker's emphasis.
segments.p2-6-g760: Что introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that'. Нельзя is again the impersonal modal 'it is not possible / one cannot'. Поддержать is the infinitive 'to support' and lacks an explicit object here because it is understood from the previous sentence (ellipsis of 'ее'). The whole subordinate clause means 'that (one) cannot support (her)'.
segments.p2-6-g761: Ну is a discourse particle meaning 'well' that signals continuation, mild impatience, or resignation; it does not affect sentence grammar.
segments.p2-6-g762: А is a connective particle 'but/and' introducing a contrast. Как here functions colloquially as a temporal/conditional 'if/when' (elliptical for 'если'). Муж 'husband' is the head noun; человек 'a person/man' is an appositive specifying the kind of man; добрый и честный are descriptive adjectives 'kind and honest' modifying человек. Удастся is the 3rd-person singular of удасться (удаться) used impersonally/antipassively meaning 'it will happen/one will manage to get' (i.e. 'if one happens to get such a husband').
segments.p2-6-g763: Так here means 'then/in that case'. Как introduces the interrogative 'how' (как тут = 'how, in that situation'); тут is an adverb meaning 'in that situation/at this point' rather than strictly spatial 'here'. Любовь is the subject 'love', and пройдет is the perfective future of пройти meaning 'will pass / fade / go away' — a rhetorical question: 'how could love pass (away)?'.
segments.p2-6-g764: Первая is the ordinal adjective 'first' modifying брачная любовь ('marital love'). Любовь is the subject and пройдет is the perfective future 'will pass/fade' (normal subject-verb order). This is a straightforward statement that the initial conjugal passion will not last.
segments.p2-6-g765: Правда here is a parenthetical discourse particle meaning 'indeed / it's true' (not the noun 'truth'); it qualifies the previous clause and is often set off by commas.
segments.p2-6-g766: А is a coordinating conjunction 'and/but' introducing the following clause. Там in this context means 'then/thereafter' (temporal), not a physical location. Придет is the future of прийти 'will come/arrive' and любовъ is the subject 'love' — 'love will come' — while еще лучше is a modifier meaning 'even better' (еще 'even/more' + лучше 'better'), describing the quality of the future love.
segments.p2-6-g749: Любовь - тайна божия uses the dash as a copular link: subject (Любовь, feminine) = predicate noun phrase (тайна божия, literally “God's/divine secret”). И introduces the second coordinated predication. От всех глаз чужих is a prepositional phrase with от governing the genitive (всех глаз), and чужих is an adjective in genitive plural modifying глаз meaning “strangers’/other people’s eyes.” Должна быть закрыта is a modal/predicative construction: должна is the short-form adjective used like a modal “ought to/must” agreeing in gender with любовь (feminine), быть is the infinitive “to be” used to form the passive, and закрыта is the short passive participle/adjectival form “closed/hidden” serving as the predicate.
segments.p2-6-g750: Что бы там ни произошло is a concessive subordinate clause meaning “whatever may happen.” Что (here functioning as a relative/interrogative element = “what/whatever”) combines with the particle бы (conditional/optative marker) and the negative particle ни to form the fixed concessive pattern что бы ни + past-tense verb. Там is a locative/particle “there/in that situation,” and произошло is the neuter past form used in this clause to mean “happen/occur” in the sense of an indefinite event.
segments.p2-6-g751: Святее is the comparative form “holier.” От этого here uses от with the genitive (этого) to indicate cause or reference — “by/for that” or “because of that,” so the phrase means literally “holier because of that.” Grammatically, the comparative adjective is used predicatively and linked to the following main comment.
segments.p2-6-g752: Лучше is the comparative/adverbial form “better” used elliptically as the sentence’s comment or conclusion; the copula (is) is omitted, so this functions as a short predicative: “(it is) better.”
segments.p2-6-g753: Друг друга is the reciprocal expression meaning “one another” (literally “friend of friend”) with second word in genitive; placed before the verb as its object. Больше modifies the verb and means “more.” Уважают is the present plural indicative of уважать (“to respect”), so the clause reads “(they) respect one another more.”
segments.p2-6-g754: А introduces a contrast/addition (“and/but”). На уважении is a prepositional phrase with на + prepositional case (уважении) meaning “on/ upon respect.” Много is an impersonal quantifier “much/a great deal.” Основано is the short passive participle used predicatively (neuter) meaning “is founded/ rests,” so the clause literally says “and on respect much is founded.”
segments.p2-6-g755: И is the simple coordinate conjunction “and.” Коль is an archaic/poetic conditional/concessive particle meaning “if” or “since”; раз уж is an emphatic combination meaning “once/now that” (раз = once, уж = already/indeed) that strengthens the conditional sense. Была is the feminine past tense of быть agreeing with любовь (feminine), so the clause means “and if once there was love / now that there once was love.”
segments.p2-6-g756: Коль again marks the conditional “if.” По любви is a prepositional phrase where по + dative/genitive usage here means “for/because of love” (motivated by love). Венчались is the past plural reflexive verb Венчаться meaning “to be married (in church)” — plural agreeing with the implied subjects; it denotes a completed event (“were wed”).
segments.p2-6-g757: Зачем introduces a rhetorical question “why/what for.” Любви is in the dative here with the infinitive проходить to form an impersonal idiomatic question — literally “for what purpose should love pass?” — i.e. “why should love pass away?” Проходить is the imperfective infinitive “to pass/go by” (here meaning to cease or fade).
segments.p2-6-g736: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking to the previous thought. так хорошо is a predicative phrase 'so good / so pleasant'; обоим (dative plural, not listed among the words) marks the experiencers 'for both of them', giving the phrase its full meaning.
segments.p2-6-g737: так хорошо вдруг станет uses станет, the 3rd person singular future of стать (perfective) meaning 'will become' or here idiomatically 'it becomes / it feels'. вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly', and the word order places the manner (так хорошо) and suddenness before the verb for emphasis.
segments.p2-6-g738: точно here is a comparative/colloquial particle meaning 'as if' rather than literal 'exactly'. вновь is 'again', они is the pronoun 'they', and встретились is the perfective past plural reflexive 'met' (they met), forming a comparative clause 'as if they had met again.'
segments.p2-6-g739: вновь повенчались is an elliptical coordinate phrase continuing the list: вновь 'again' + повенчались, past plural perfective reflexive of повенчаться, 'were married / were joined in church again' (a church wedding sense).
segments.p2-6-g740: вновь (again) modifies the clause любовь у них началась. началась is past feminine singular (agreeing with любовь) of начаться, perfective 'began'. у них is a possessive/experiencer construction 'with them / of theirs' so the literal structure is 'love began with them' = 'their love began.' Word order places вновь and любовь to emphasize renewal.
segments.p2-6-g741: И is the coordinating 'and'. никто is the negative pronoun 'no one'. The enclitic -то attached (никто-то) is emphatic/contrastive here ('no one at all' / 'nobody, mind you'), adding force to the negation.
segments.p2-6-g742: никто-то не должен знать: не должен is a negative modal construction meaning 'must not / ought not' (должен used modally). знать is the infinitive 'to know'; the subject (никто-то) governs the modal phrase, so the literal structure is 'no one ought to know.'
segments.p2-6-g743: что introduces the subordinate clause object of 'знать' ('what'). между governs the instrumental pair мужем и женой ('between husband and wife' — note the use of instrumental after между). происходит is present tense 3rd person singular of происходить 'is happening / occurs', used impersonally of the situation unfolding between them.
segments.p2-6-g744: коль is a colloquial conditional 'if' (not causal here). они любят is straightforward present-tense 'they love', and друг друга is the standard reciprocal expression 'one another' (друг used in the sense 'other/each other' here, with друга as its genitive/reciprocal partner).
segments.p2-6-g745: И 'and' links the sentence. какая ... бы ни вышла is a concessive conditional construction meaning 'whatever ... may arise'; какая is 'what/which', бы is the modal/conditional particle, ни is the negative particle used in such concessive phrases, and вышла is past feminine singular of выйти used here in the sense 'turn out / arise' agreeing with ссора (feminine). у них indicates 'between them / among them' (locative/possessive sense).
segments.p2-6-g746: мать родную is an accusative NP 'the (one's) own mother' with родную (agreeing feminine accusative) as an emotive/possessive adjective 'own/dear', emphasizing closeness; the phrase sets up the object that follows the prohibition.
segments.p2-6-g747: и ту ('and that one' — tu refers back to the mother) не должны себе в судьи звать is 'should not call (her) to themselves as judges': не должны is negative modal 'should not/ought not', себе is dative reflexive 'for themselves' (marking 'to their own benefit/for themselves'), в судьи is a role-expression 'as judges' (в + noun indicating capacity), and звать is the infinitive 'to call/summon'. The second coordinated infinitive и ... рассказывать continues the prohibition: 'and (they) should not tell one about the other' (про + другого 'about the other').
segments.p2-6-g748: Сами is an intensive/reflexive pronoun 'themselves' placed before the subject for emphasis. There is no copula in present-tense Russian, so the predicate судьи (plural noun) stands as the nominal predicate meaning 'are judges'. себе is the dative reflexive 'for themselves', so the whole sentence reads 'they themselves are judges to/for themselves.'
segments.p2-6-g851: К концу-то речи — a temporal prepositional phrase. К + dative (концу) means “by/toward the end”; the clitic particle то after концу is colloquial, adding emphasis (‘by the very end’). Речи is genitive singular after к концу and names what is ending (речь = “speech”). Я действительно разгорячился is the main clause: я (subject) + действительно (adverb: 'really/actually') + разгорячился (past tense, perfective/reflexive verb meaning ‘became heated/got worked up’), so the clause says that I grew heated by that time.
segments.p2-6-g852: И теперь самолюбие как-то страдало — и joins this clause to the previous one. Теперь = 'now' (temporal). Самолюбие is a neuter noun 'self‑esteem/pride' as subject. Как-то is an adverbial use of как + particle-то meaning 'somehow' or 'in a way'; it modifies the verb. Страдало is past tense, imperfective, neuter singular to agree with самолюбие, meaning 'was suffering' or 'suffered' (ongoing/experiential).
segments.p2-6-g853: Simple, two-word clause: Молчание (neuter noun, nominative subject 'silence') + длилось (past tense, neuter singular of длиться, imperfective, 'to last, continue'). The verb agrees in gender/number with the neuter subject and indicates duration in the past.
segments.p2-6-g854: Я (subject) + даже (adverb 'even', adds emphasis) + хотел (past tense, masculine singular of хотеть, 'wanted') + толкнуть (perfective infinitive 'to push') + её (accusative object pronoun 'her'). The past-tense хотеть + infinitive expresses a past desire or inclination; the object pronoun её is unstressed and follows the infinitive as its direct object.
segments.p2-6-g855: Чтой-то вы is a colloquial, truncated interrogative. Чтой (an informal variant of что) is the interrogative 'what', and the enclitic то softens/qualifies it (common in spoken Russian). The verb is omitted but implied, so the line is an elliptical question addressed to вы ('you', polite or plural). The whole functions as a short reproach or puzzled question: 'What are you (at)?' or 'What are you doing?'
segments.p2-6-g767: Там is an adverb meaning 'there' (locative/temporal), placed first for emphasis. душой is the noun душа in the instrumental singular used adverbially — 'in spirit/with their hearts'. сойдутся is the 3rd person plural future (perfective) of сойтись, a reciprocal verb meaning 'will come together; unite'.
segments.p2-6-g768: все дела свои is a noun phrase where все (all) + дела (affairs) + the reflexive possessive свои (their own) indicate 'all their affairs'. сообща is an adverb 'together/collectively'. положут is 3rd person plural future of положить ('put/lay'), here idiomatically 'place together' or 'pool'.
segments.p2-6-g769: тайны is 'secrets' (plural) and is the topic whose absence is asserted; друг от друга is a fixed reciprocal expression 'from one another' (друг ... друга = each other). не будет is the impersonal future construction 'there will not be' indicating absence.
segments.p2-6-g770: А is a simple linking particle 'and' / 'but' starting the sentence. дети is the plural subject 'children'. пойдут is 3rd person plural future of пойти; in this context it means 'will come/arrive/will start to appear' (children will come into the family or life), a sense of beginning/coming into being.
segments.p2-6-g771: так тут каждое is a small clause meaning 'so/then here every (one)'; так 'so/in that case', тут 'here/at this point', and каждое is a neuter pronoun 'every/each' anticipating the noun (время) that follows.
segments.p2-6-g772: хоть и is a concessive conjunction 'even if/though' (here 'even'), самое трудное время is a superlative noun phrase 'the very hardest time', счастьем is the instrumental of счастие used as a predicative complement 'as happiness', and покажется is the 3rd person singular future of показаться 'will appear/seem'.
segments.p2-6-g773: только бы is an optative/conditional formula meaning 'if only / provided that' with бы marking wish or hypothetical. любить and быть are infinitives 'to love' and 'to be', joined by the archaic coordinating particle да functioning as 'and' between infinitives; мужественным is the predicate adjective 'brave/courageous'.
segments.p2-6-g774: Тут 'here/at this point', и is an emphatic 'even/also' (adds emphasis), работа is the nominative 'work', and весела is the short-form adjective (feminine) used predicatively to mean 'is cheerful/pleasant'.
segments.p2-6-g775: This clause has an idiomatic structure: тут и в хлебе себе иной раз отказываешь для детей — 'here you even in bread deny yourself at times for the children'. в хлебе (literally 'in bread') means 'in terms of bread/food/basic sustenance', себе is dative reflexive 'to yourself/for yourself', иной раз is an adverbial 'sometimes', отказываешь is 2nd person singular present 'you deny/you refuse yourself', and для детей marks purpose 'for the children'.
segments.p2-6-g776: и то is a resumptive phrase 'and that/that too' referring back to the foregoing, and весело is the short-form neuter predicate 'is joyful/cheerful'.
segments.p2-6-g777: Ведь is an emphatic particle 'after all/you see', они 'they', ж is an adding/emphatic particle reinforcing the assertion, тебя is the accusative 'you' (object), будут ... любить is the analytic future 'will love', за это 'for this' gives the reason, and потом 'later'.
segments.p2-6-g778: себе is the dative/reflexive pronoun 'for oneself/for your own sake' (indicating the beneficiary of the action), and же is an emphatic/contrastive particle here adding a slight 'after all/on the other hand' sense.
segments.p2-6-g779: значит is a parenthetical/conclusive particle 'it means/so/therefore' used colloquially to draw the inference from the previous clause.
segments.p2-6-g780: копишь is 2nd person singular present of копить 'you save / accumulate (money or resources)'; it is a simple statement about what the subject is doing (colloquial, elliptic after the previous short phrases).
segments.p2-6-g892: Но is an adversative particle meaning 'but' at the sentence start. Я is the first-person pronoun; не negates the verb that follows. Догадался is the past tense, masculine, perfective form of догадаться (the reflexive/‑ся verb), here meaning 'to come to a guess/realize' — the perfective past emphasizes a single, completed act (which did not occur because of не). The comma ends this clause and prepares the coordinate clause introduced by и.
segments.p2-6-g893: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this clause to the previous one. Злое is an adjective in nominative neuter singular agreeing with чувство; here it conveys hostile/spiteful quality. Чувство is the neuter noun 'feeling' (nominative subject). Обхватило is the past tense, neuter singular form of обхватить (perfective) and agrees with чувство (neuter) — used figuratively as 'seized' or 'came over' (an emotion seizing someone). Меня is the accusative pronoun 'me', the direct object of the verbal feeling-seizing.
segments.p2-6-g894: Постой is an imperative form meaning 'wait' or 'hold on' (colloquial imperative of стоять/постоять); it appears inside quotation marks as the narrator's internal or spoken remark. Же is an emphatic particle here, strengthening the imperative ('do wait' / 'wait then'). The comma closes the quoted utterance before the speech tag.
segments.p2-6-g895: The dash separates the quoted utterance from the reporting clause. Подумал is the past tense, masculine form of подумать ('thought'); placing it after the quote is the normal Russian order for a speech/thought tag. Я is the subject 'I'; the masculine past form of the verb reveals the narrator's gender.
segments.p2-6-g841: "Картинками" is the plural noun картинка in the instrumental case (картинками), used adverbially to mark means or manner: "by/with pictures." The initial comma and placement give it emphatic, exclamatory force in direct speech.
segments.p2-6-g842: вот этими-то картинками — a demonstrative+instrumental cluster: вот is a pointing particle ('right/these'), этими is the demonstrative 'these' in instrumental plural, and the enclitic -то adds emphasis ('these very'). Картинками repeats the instrumental noun to show means; тебя is the accusative object ('you'); надо is the impersonal modal 'one must/it's necessary' with the accusative object (colloquially meaning 'you need to be [dealt with]'). The word order is colloquial and emphatic: instrument first, then the object and modal.
segments.p2-6-g843: подумал я про себя — подумал is past tense, masculine, perfective (I thought), я is the subject (moved after the verb for a slightly emphatic or conversational tone), про себя is an adverbial phrase meaning 'to myself' or 'inwardly.' Together: 'I thought to myself.'
segments.p2-6-g844: хотя — the conjunction 'although' introducing a concessive clause; here it signals contrast with what immediately follows.
segments.p2-6-g845: ей-богу — an interjectional oath meaning 'I swear to God' or 'honestly'; grammatically it's the dative form 'ей' + 'богу' fused into a colloquial exclamation rather than a literal dative phrase.
segments.p2-6-g846: с чувством говорил — с + instrumental (чувством) marks manner: 'with feeling.' Говорил is the past imperfective 'spoke/was speaking' (first person implied from context), so 'I spoke with feeling' or 'I was speaking feelingly.'
segments.p2-6-g847: и вдруг покраснел — и 'and'; вдруг 'suddenly'; покраснел is the perfective past 'blushed/turned red' (himself became red). The clause states a sudden bodily reaction following the previous remark.
segments.p2-6-g848: А ну если она вдруг расхохочется — А is a contrasting conjunction ('but/and'), ну is a colloquial particle softening or prompting ('well/now'), если introduces the condition 'if', она is the subject 'she', вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly' (or 'what if suddenly'), and расхохочется is the perfective future 3rd person singular of расхохотаться 'to burst into loud laughter.' The clause is a hypothetical: 'But suppose she suddenly bursts out laughing...'.
segments.p2-6-g849: куда я тогда полезу? — куда 'to where'; я subject; тогда 'then/in that case'; полезу is first person future of полезть (literally 'I will climb'), used colloquially as 'where will I go/what will I do (then)?' — here meaning 'how will I get out of that situation / where will I stick my neck?'
segments.p2-6-g850: Эта идея меня привела в бешенство — Эта (demonstrative) modifies идея (nominative subject). Привела is past feminine singular of привести ('to lead/bring'), agreeing with идея; меня is the accusative object 'me'. The phrase в бешенство ('into a rage') is a result/complement introduced by в + noun in accusative; the whole construction means 'this idea led me / caused me to become furious.'
segments.p2-6-g825: "А то" is a colloquial connective meaning "or else/and then" that introduces a sudden or likely action; "возьмет" is the 3rd person singular future perfective of взять, here idiomatically 'he will take/grab' or 'he'll just (do it)'. The comma shows the clause leads into what he then does.
segments.p2-6-g826: "да и" is an intensifying connective meaning "and even/and then"; "прикусит" is 3rd person singular future of прикусить (perfective) meaning 'will bite down (on)'; "матери грудь" reads as грудь (accusative direct object) modified by матери (genitive) — "mother's breast."
segments.p2-6-g827: "коль уж" is a somewhat colloquial/older connective meaning "now that/since"; "зубки" is the diminutive plural 'little teeth' and "прорезываются" is the present reflexive form meaning 'are cutting through / are coming in' (teeth coming through).
segments.p2-6-g828: "а сам" contrasts with the previous action and emphasizes 'and he himself'; "глазенками" is a diminutive instrumental plural ('with his little eyes') indicating manner; the particle "то" here is emphatic; "косит" (present) means 'looks askance/squints' or 'steals a sideways glance'; "на нее" is the preposition + pronoun 'at her'.
segments.p2-6-g829: "Видишь" is 2nd person singular present of видеть used rhetorically as 'see?/look — you see', addressing the listener and drawing attention to the fact stated.
segments.p2-6-g830: "прикусил" is the past tense (masculine) of прикусить 'bit (down)' used in direct speech as the child's triumphant exclamation 'I bit (you)!' The subject is omitted because it's spoken utterance.
segments.p2-6-g831: "Да разве не" opens a rhetorical/negatively phrased question meaning 'isn't it...?' or 'surely it is...?' ; "все тут счастье" literally 'all here happiness' means 'complete happiness here' with все used as an intensifier modifying счастье; word order puts emphasis on the totality of the happiness.
segments.p2-6-g832: "когда" means 'when' introducing the temporal clause; "они трое" is a pronoun plus the collective numeral трое meaning 'the three of them' (трое used for counting people), placed after они for emphasis/clarity.
segments.p2-6-g833: "муж" is nominative singular 'husband', given as the first item in the appositive list that specifies who the three are.
segments.p2-6-g834: "жена и ребенок" continues the appositive list: "жена" 'wife', conjunction "и" 'and', and "ребенок" 'child'; standard nominative forms in a simple list.
segments.p2-6-g835: "вместе" is an adverb meaning 'together' and closes the question by specifying the circumstance in which that complete happiness occurs.
segments.p2-6-g836: "За эти минуты" uses the preposition за with a time expression to mean 'during/for these minutes'; the impersonal construction "много можно простить" places the quantifier "много" ('a lot') before the modal "можно" and infinitive "простить" to mean 'many things can be forgiven' or 'one can forgive a great deal', with no explicit subject.
segments.p2-6-g837: A short interjection meaning a flat refusal or correction; here it serves as an emphatic 'No,' introducing the speaker's objection.
segments.p2-6-g838: A vocative: the addressee's name set off by a comma to address Liza directly; no grammatical change to the rest of the sentence.
segments.p2-6-g839: A compact clause: the particle/parenthetical 'знать' (used colloquially here as a modal remark 'you know/it seems'), followed by 'самому' (dative/emphatic 'oneself/yourself'), temporal adverb 'сначала' ('first'), impersonal 'нужно' ('it is necessary'), the infinitive 'жить' ('to live'), and the perfective 'выучиться' ('learn'—here in the infinitive form agreeing with 'нужно'). The word order places the modal/introductory particle first and then the necessity construction 'самому... нужно ... выучиться' ('you yourself need to learn to live').
segments.p2-6-g840: Coordinating clause started with 'а' ('and/then/but') linking contrast; 'потом' ('afterwards/then'), intensifier 'уж' (emphatic 'already/at that point'), 'других' (accusative plural 'others/other people'), and the infinitive 'обвинять' ('to accuse/blame'). Together: 'and only after that blame others.'
segments.p2-6-g870: Да here is an interjection/particle expressing mild surprise or emphasis (not a literal affirmative 'yes'). Вы is the vocative/address form 'you' (nominative used for direct address, formal or plural). The whole short utterance is an exclamatory, elliptical remark directed at the interlocutor.
segments.p2-6-g873: Что is an interrogative pronoun used alone as a one-word question; the verb and other elements are omitted and the rising intonation (in speech) makes it a direct question meaning 'What?'.
segments.p2-6-g874: Что-то is the indefinite pronoun formed by что + то ('something' / 'somewhat') used colloquially to evaluate or dismiss; вы is the addressee. Together this elliptical tag means roughly 'You're something' or 'There's something about you' and relies on context and tone to convey judgment.
segments.p2-6-g877: точно is an adverb meaning 'exactly' or 'just'; как introduces a simile 'like/as'; по + dative (по книге) means 'in/according to the book' or 'as in the book.' The phrase functions as a simile modifying how she spoke.
segments.p2-6-g878: сказала is the past tense, feminine singular of 'to say' and serves as the speech tag for the preceding direct speech; она is the subject 'she'. The comma and preceding dash mark the end of quoted speech and attribute it to her.
segments.p2-6-g879: This clause is built around the impersonal/passive-seeming verb послышалось ('was heard' / 'seemed to be heard'). Что-то насмешливое is an indefinite phrase 'something mocking' (что-то + adjective used substantively); как будто 'as if'; вдруг 'suddenly'; опять 'again'; в её голосе is the prepositional phrase 'in her voice' specifying where it was heard.
segments.p2-6-g720: Уйдет is the perfective future, 3rd person singular of уйти ('to leave'), placed before the pronoun for emphasis; the clause reads as a hypothetical/conditional 'if he leaves/goes off somewhere'. 'он' is the subject 'he', and 'куда' is an adverbial 'to somewhere/to where' (here indefinite).
segments.p2-6-g721: This parenthetical clause uses past tense 'знал' ('I knew') with 'одну' (feminine accusative 'one') where the noun is omitted – i.e. 'I knew one (such woman)'. The narrator's past-tense verb frames the example he is about to give.
segments.p2-6-g722: Negative particle 'не' plus the future-perfective verb 'стерпит' (3rd sg of стерпеть) yields 'she will not endure/tolerate it'. The subject is omitted but understood from context (the woman just mentioned).
segments.p2-6-g723: The initial 'да' here is a colloquial connective/emphatic 'and/even'; 'в самую ночь' is temporal — 'in the very night' (самую = 'the very' modifying ночь); 'и' is a simple conjunction 'and'; 'выскочит' is future-perfective, 3rd sg of выскочить meaning 'she will bolt/jump out suddenly'.
segments.p2-6-g724: 'Да и' together functions as an intensifying connective ('and then/even'); 'бежит' is present tense, 3rd sg of бежать (imperfective) used here in vivid narration — 'she runs off'; 'потихоньку' is an adverb meaning 'quietly/stealthily'; 'смотреть' is the infinitive of purpose: 'to look/to see (to check)'.
segments.p2-6-g725: This is a short rhetorical question formed with 'не... ли' — 'не' (negation) + enclitic 'ли' making it interrogative; 'там' means 'there'. Together: 'isn't he there?'.
segments.p2-6-g726: 'в том доме' is a locative expression ('in that house') with 'в' as the locative/prepositional preposition and 'том' the demonstrative 'that'; adding enclitic 'ли' turns it into the question 'isn't it in that house?'.
segments.p2-6-g727: Another short interrogative with 'не... ли': 'с той' = 'with that one' (с + feminine demonstrative той), so the clause asks 'isn't he with that [woman]?'. 'С' here means 'with/from' in the sense 'with'.
segments.p2-6-g728: 'Это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/that/it' as the subject; 'уж' is an emphatic particle ('really/indeed' here); 'худо' is a short predicative meaning 'bad'/'badly'. The three together form a compact evaluative statement: 'This is really bad.'
segments.p2-6-g729: 'И' ('and') connects this clause; 'сама' is a reflexive/emphatic pronoun 'herself' (emphasizing that she personally knows); 'знает' is present tense, 3rd sg of знать — 'she knows'.
segments.p2-6-g730: 'что' is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause; 'худо' is the predicative 'bad' — together: 'that it is bad.'
segments.p2-6-g731: This clause has coordinated predicates about her feelings: 'сердце у ней' is a possessive construction 'her heart' ('сердце' as subject with 'у ней' marking possession); 'замирает' is present 'stops/halts' (here 'the heart skips/stops'); the second verb 'казнится' is reflexive and means 'is punished/torments itself' (i.e. she tortures herself or feels self-reproach). The two verbs are linked by the coordinating 'и'.
segments.p2-6-g732: 'да ведь' is colloquial — 'да' as a connective plus 'ведь' ('after all') gives a concessive/emphatic 'and yet/after all'; 'любит' is present 3rd sg of любить — 'she loves.'
segments.p2-6-g733: 'все' here means 'everything' or 'all of it'; 'от' is the preposition 'from/because of'; 'любви' is the genitive of 'любовь' — 'all because of love' (a causal phrase).
segments.p2-6-g734: 'А' is a connective particle 'and/but'; 'как хорошо' is an exclamative phrase 'how nice/good'; 'после ссоры' is temporal 'after the quarrel'; 'помириться' is the perfective infinitive 'to reconcile/make up' — the whole functions as an impersonal exclamation about the pleasure of reconciling after a quarrel.
segments.p2-6-g735: 'самой' emphasizes that she herself does it ('to humble herself'); 'перед ним' means 'in front of/before him' (перед + pronoun in the appropriate form); 'повиниться' is an infinitive meaning 'to confess/submit' (humble oneself); 'али' is an archaic or literary coordinative 'or'; 'простить' is the infinitive 'to forgive'. The sequence lists two possible conciliatory actions she might take.
segments.p2-6-g799: "Знаешь" is a colloquial filler meaning "you know" (addressing the listener). The phrase "розовенький такой мальчик" uses diminutive/affectionate morphology (‑еньк‑, ‑ок) and the particle "такой" as an intensifier: literally "such a rosy little boy."
segments.p2-6-g800: "грудь" is the direct object (here: "the breast"), "тебе" is dative indicating possession/association with the addressee ("your"), and "сосет" is 3rd person singular present indicative of "сосать" describing the baby nursing.
segments.p2-6-g801: Here "да" functions as an archaic coordinating particle (= "and/and yet"). "у какого мужа" uses "у" + genitive to mean "in/for what (kind of) husband," with "какого" rhetorical. "сердце повернется" uses the reflexive perfective "повернется" (3rd sg.) meaning "the heart will turn" or "will be moved toward." "на жену" indicates direction/target (toward the wife).
segments.p2-6-g802: "глядя" is the adverbial participle (деепричастие) of "глядеть," meaning "(while) looking" or "seeing," and links the observation to the following clause as simultaneous/causal.
segments.p2-6-g803: "как" introduces a manner clause "how/as," "она" is the subject "she," "с + инструментал" ("с его ребенком") means "with his child," and "сидит" (3rd sg. present) describes the posture "sits/holds." The whole clause = "when/seeing how she sits with his child."
segments.p2-6-g804: "Ребеночек" is the diminutive of "ребёнок," expressing affection ("little baby"), and "розовенький" is a diminutive/adjective meaning "rosy/pinkish," forming a warm descriptive opening.
segments.p2-6-g805: "пухленький" is a diminutive adjective meaning "plump" or "chubby," used affectionately to describe the baby's build.
segments.p2-6-g806: "раскинется" is future/perfective 3rd singular of "раскинуться," here describing the baby "spreading himself out" or stretching out comfortably.
segments.p2-6-g807: "нежится" (3rd sg. present) from "нежиться" means "basks/relaxes/ luxuriates," showing pampered, contented behavior.
segments.p2-6-g808: "ножки-ручки" are paired diminutives (little legs and little hands); "наливные" is a figurative adjective meaning "plump/juicy/full" (like ripe fruit), describing their rounded, full appearance.
segments.p2-6-g809: "ноготочки" is the diminutive plural "little nails," and "чистенькие" is a diminutive/adverbial form meaning "nice and clean" or "very clean" with affectionate tone.
segments.p2-6-g810: "маленькие" is a straightforward plural adjective "small/tiny," repeating the idea of smallness.
segments.p2-6-g811: "такие" intensifies the adjective that follows ("such/sotruly"), so "такие маленькие" = "so/that small," emphasizing the tiny size.
segments.p2-6-g812: "что" here introduces a result clause ("so... that..."), "глядеть" is impersonal infinitive meaning "to look (at)" or "to be looked at," and "смешно" is a predicative adjective "funny/odd," so the clause means "so small that it's funny to look at them."
segments.p2-6-g813: "глазки" is the diminutive plural of "глаза" (little eyes) used affectionately; it's a nominative tag leading into the next clause about their expression.
segments.p2-6-g814: "точно" here functions like "as if," "уж" is an intensifier ("already/indeed"), "он" is the subject "he," "все" means "everything," and "понимает" (3rd sg. present) means "understands" — together: "as if he already understands everything."
segments.p2-6-g815: "А" is a coordinating particle here (= "and/while"); "сосет" is 3rd sg. present "sucks/suckles." The dash signals a shift to a simultaneous action: "грудь тебе" (accusative + dative) indicates "your breast," "ручонкой" is instrumental singular diminutive "with his tiny hand," and "теребит" (3rd sg.) means "fidgets with/picks at."
segments.p2-6-g816: "играет" (3rd sg. present of "играть") means "plays" or "is playing," here indicating a playful, light action accompanying the nursing.
segments.p2-6-g817: Отец подойдет — simple future, 3rd-person singular of the perfective подойти 'to approach / come up'. It states a single forthcoming action by the father.
segments.p2-6-g818: оторвется от груди — оторвется is perfective reflexive future (3rd person) 'will tear/tear oneself away'; от + genitive груди marks separation from the source, here 'from the breast'.
segments.p2-6-g819: перегнется весь назад — перегнется is perfective reflexive future 'will bend/arch back'; весь 'whole/all' intensifies the motion and назад ('backwards') gives direction, so 'arch all the way back.'
segments.p2-6-g820: посмотрит на отца — посмотрит is perfective future (3rd person) 'will look (at)'; the preposition на + accusative отца indicates the target of the look ('at his father').
segments.p2-6-g821: засмеется — perfective future (3rd person) 'will burst into / begin to laugh'; used as a single action following the look.
segments.p2-6-g822: точно уж и бог знает как смешно — a parenthetical emphatic clause: точно уж ('really / indeed') + и (filler 'and / even') leading into the idiom бог знает как 'God knows how' to mark an indeterminate high degree, with смешно as the predicative 'funny'. Together: 'it's really, God knows how funny.'
segments.p2-6-g823: и опять, — a brief connective: и 'and' plus опять 'again', the repetition serving to emphasize the recurrence of the action that follows.
segments.p2-6-g824: опять сосать примется — примется is perfective reflexive future 'will set about / begin' and takes the infinitive сосать 'to suck'; опять preceding both verbs emphasizes that the sucking resumes again (word order places the adverb first for emphasis).
segments.p2-6-g880: This whole clause is a simple Russian impersonal/experiential perfective construction: the past verb ущипнуло (neuter singular) takes an experiencer in the accusative (меня) and a nominative subject expressing what caused the sensation (это замечанье). Больно is an adverb modifying the verb, emphatically marking the sensation as painful. Word order (adverb–verb–object–subject) is natural and slightly colloquial/evocative here.
segments.p2-6-g881: Я (I) is the subject, and the predicate is ожидал in the past masculine singular. Не negates the expectation. Того is a genitive demonstrative used here elliptically (not the neutral это) to mean "that (thing/way)" — the construction Я не того ожидал = "I wasn't expecting that" (literal: "I expected not-that").
segments.p2-6-g882: Я is the subject; и here functions as an intensifier meaning "even" (common in colloquial literary Russian: Я и не понял = "I didn't even understand/realize"). Не negates the verb понял (past masculine of понять), here best rendered "realize."
segments.p2-6-g883: This subordinate clause begins with the complementizer что ("that"). Она is the subject; нарочно (adverb) means "deliberately." Маскировалась is imperfective reflexive past meaning "masked herself/put on a disguise"; the preposition в + accusative насмешку indicates the guise she assumed: "masked herself as mockery" or "affected mockery."
segments.p2-6-g884: Another "that" clause: что это is "that this was..." Обыкновенная последняя уловка is a noun phrase meaning "a common/final trick" (обыкновенная = ordinary/common; последняя = last/final). Уловка is a feminine noun in nominative agreeing with obyknoвенная and последняя.
segments.p2-6-g885: This relative clause (которым ... и которые ...) modifies "людей." Которым is dative plural "to whom" and introduces the description: грубо и навязчиво лезут в душу — present plural verb лезут (лезть) meaning "intrude" or "poke into," with adverbs грубо (rudely) and навязчиво (intrusively). The second relative pronoun которые (nominative plural) picks up the same people and continues with до последней минуты ("until the last minute") + negated present сдаются ("do not surrender/give in") от гордости ("from pride"), and боятся перед вами высказать — "are afraid before you to express" their feeling (высказать свое чувство). Note the sequence switches from dative description to nominative clauses describing the same class of people.
segments.p2-6-g886: Уже here means "already" (temporal/emphatic). По + noun in this idiomatic phrase (по робости) indicates cause or manner: "because of/from her timidity" or "given her timidity." Робости is the genitive of робость (timidity), governed by по in this usage.
segments.p2-6-g887: С которой is a relative phrase "with which/with which (timidity)" referring back to робости; она is the subject and приступала is the imperfective past of приступать meaning "began/approached (something)" — here "she began/started (her mockery)". The preposition + relative pronoun link the manner of beginning to the timidity mentioned.
segments.p2-6-g888: В несколько приемов is an adverbial phrase meaning "in several attempts / in several moves/steps." Приемов is genitive plural after в with a quantified expression and here denotes separate "moves/attempts/techniques."
segments.p2-6-g889: К своей насмешке: к + dative (своей насмешке) indicates "toward her mockery" or "in regard to her mockery" — i.e., the object she was approaching in those several attempts. Своя agrees in gender/case/number with насмешке (feminine dative).
segments.p2-6-g890: И наконец только решилась высказать: и наконец = "and finally"; только here intensifies and means "only/merely/just" in the sense "only at the end did she then dare." Решилась is perfective reflexive past of решиться meaning "decided/dared (to)" — often used to mean "found the courage to." Высказать is the infinitive complement "to express."
segments.p2-6-g891: This main clause is a counterfactual/modal evaluation in the past: я бы должен был догадаться. Я is the subject; бы is the conditional particle (marks what should/hypothetically have been the case); должен был is the modal phrase "ought to have"/"should have" (должен + past быть); догадаться is the perfective infinitive "to guess/figure out." The whole means "I should have guessed."
segments.p2-6-g858: Here the past-tense verb начала (fem.) means “began” (verb + subject inversion: verb before она for a slightly emphatic or narrative effect). Вдруг is an adverb “suddenly” modifying начала, and и connects a second past reflexive verb остановилась “stopped/paused” (also past, feminine), so the clause describes two successive actions. The leading dash marks reported speech or a break in dialogue.
segments.p2-6-g859: Simple main clause: conjunction 'Но' ('But'), subject 'я' ('I'), adverb 'уже' ('already'), object/adverbial 'всё' ('everything') and past tense verb 'понял' (masculine singular past of 'понять' — 'understood'). The colon signals that what follows explains or exemplifies that understanding.
segments.p2-6-g860: Prepositional phrase 'в её голосе' ('in her voice') followed by 'уже' ('already') and the indefinite subject 'что-то' ('something') plus 'другое' ('else/other') and the past imperfective verb 'дрожало' ('was trembling'). Note the verb is neuter singular because 'что-то' is treated as a neuter singular pronoun.
segments.p2-6-g861: Negative phrase: particle 'не' negates the adjective 'резкое' ('sharp'); the adjective is neuter singular to agree with the neuter 'что-то' introduced earlier.
segments.p2-6-g862: Continues the negative description: 'не' negates 'грубое' ('rough/crude'), 'и' links a second adjectival/participle description 'несдающееся' ('unyielding' — present participle/verbal adjective formed from 'сдаваться' with negative prefix). Both adjectives agree in gender/number with the neuter subject.
segments.p2-6-g863: Comparative adverbial phrase: 'как' ('as/like') introduces the comparison and 'недавно' ('recently') places the contrasted quality in recent past reference ('as it had been recently').
segments.p2-6-g864: Contrastive clause introduced by 'а' ('but/rather'): again the indefinite 'что-то' ('something') + adjectives 'мягкое' ('soft/gentle') and 'стыдливое' ('bashful/shy'), both neuter to agree with 'что-то'.
segments.p2-6-g865: 'До того' is an idiomatic degree phrase meaning 'to such a degree' or 'so ... that', followed by the adjective 'стыдливое' ('shy'); it sets up the ensuing result clause introduced by 'что'.
segments.p2-6-g866: Result clause introduced by 'что' ('that'): 'мне' (dative 'to me') plus emphatic 'самому' ('myself') indicate who experiences the feeling; 'как-то вдруг' is an adverbial combination ('somehow/suddenly') and 'перед ней' ('in front of her') is a prepositional phrase marking the social context. The impersonal predicate 'стыдно стало' uses the predicative adjective 'стыдно' with the verb 'стало' ('became') to mean 'I felt ashamed'.
segments.p2-6-g867: A short final clause repeating the emotional result: 'виновато' (adverbial predicative 'guiltily/feeling guilty') plus 'стало' ('became') — another impersonal construction indicating the speaker's subjective feeling.
segments.p2-6-g868: The initial dash marks direct speech. Что is an interrogative pronoun used alone as a one-word question meaning 'What?'; the question mark indicates interrogative intonation and that the speaker requests clarification or repetition.
segments.p2-6-g869: The verb спросил is the past tense, perfective form of спросить (masculine singular), placed before the pronoun я (inverted order for stylistic or emphatic effect); together they mean “I asked.” The prepositional phrase с + instrumental (с нежным любопытством) uses с with the instrumental case (нежным modifies любопытством) to indicate manner — “with gentle/tender curiosity.” The initial dash is a dialogue marker and does not affect the internal grammar.
